items.abstract_zh: 模板计算是各种 HPC 和智能计算应用程序的基础，通常会消耗大量的执行时间。专用矩阵单元的出现为加速模板计算提供了新的机会。虽然可扩展矩阵计算单元提供了强大的计算能力，但由于矩阵单元利用率不理想、指令级并行性有限以及缓存命中率低，先前的努力未能充分利用模板的计算能力。本文介绍了 HStencil，一种利用矩阵和向量单元的新型模板计算框架。 HStencil 通过三个贡献来应对这些挑战：1）联合利用矩阵和向量单元来提高硬件利用率的微内核； 2）采用交错执行的细粒度指令调度，增强指令级并行性； 3) 空间预取，以在工作集超出缓存容量时维持高性能。对代表性基准的评估表明，HStencil 在不同 CPU 平台上比自动矢量化实现了 1.81 倍至 5.76 倍的最大加速，与最先进的方法相比，性能提高了 31% - 91%。

items.title: MANS: Efficient and Portable ANS Encoding for Multi-Byte Integer Data on CPUs and GPUs.
items.abstract: Lossless compression is a classic technique for reducing data storage and transmission requirements. Asymmetric Numeral Systems (ANS) is a high-throughput, high-ratio lossless compression algorithm, but it lacks effective support for multi-byte data and cross-platform compatibility. To address this issue, we propose an Adaptive Data Mapping (ADM) scheme, which maps multi-byte integer data into single-byte space based on the data’s characteristics, improving the compression ratio of ANS while maintaining low encoding redundancy. We also optimize the ADM algorithm and the ANS encoder for GPU and CPU architectures, respectively, and combine them to create an efficient and portable ANS encoding method for multi-byte integer data, called MANS. Experimental results show that MANS improves compression ratios by an average of 1.24 ×, achieves 870.27MB/s throughput on CPUs, and delivers up to 288.45 × and 135.86 × speedups on an NVIDIA A100 and an AMD MI210 GPU compared to the CPU version—demonstrating its efficiency and portability across platforms.
items.abstract_zh: 无损压缩是降低数据存储和传输要求的经典技术。非对称数字系统（ANS）是一种高吞吐量、高比率的无损压缩算法，但缺乏对多字节数据的有效支持和跨平台兼容性。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种自适应数据映射（ADM）方案，该方案根据数据的特性将多字节整数数据映射到单字节空间，提高ANS的压缩率，同时保持低编码冗余。我们还分别针对 GPU 和 CPU 架构优化了 ADM 算法和 ANS 编码器，并将它们结合起来创建了一种针对多字节整数数据的高效且可移植的 ANS 编码方法，称为 MANS。实验结果表明，与 CPU 版本相比，MANS 平均将压缩比提高了 1.24 倍，在 CPU 上实现了 870.27MB/s 的吞吐量，在 NVIDIA A100 和 AMD MI210 GPU 上提供了高达 288.45 倍和 135.86 倍的加速，展示了其跨平台的效率和可移植性。

items.title: Numerical Performance of the Implicitly Restarted Arnoldi Method in OFP8, Bfloat16, Posit, and Takum Arithmetics.
items.abstract: The computation of select eigenvalues and eigenvectors of large, sparse matrices is fundamental to a wide range of applications. Accordingly, evaluating the numerical performance of emerging alternatives to the IEEE 754 floating-point standard, such as OFP8 (E4M3 and E5M2), bfloat16, and the tapered-precision posit and takum formats, is of significant interest. Among the most widely used methods for this task is the implicitly restarted Arnoldi method, as implemented in ARPACK.
items.abstract_zh: 大型稀疏矩阵的选择特征值和特征向量的计算是广泛应用的基础。因此，评估 IEEE 754 浮点标准的新兴替代方案（例如 OFP8（E4M3 和 E5M2）、bfloat16 以及锥形精度 posit 和 takum 格式）的数值性能具有重要意义。此任务最广泛使用的方法之一是隐式重新启动的 Arnoldi 方法，如 ARPACK 中实现的那样。

items.title: Improving SpGEMM Performance Through Matrix-Reordering and Cluster-wise Computation.
items.abstract: Sparse matrix-sparse matrix multiplication (SpGEMM) is a key kernel in many scientific applications and graph workloads. Unfortunately, SpGEMM is bottlenecked by data movement due to its irregular memory access patterns. Significant work has been devoted to developing row reordering schemes towards improving locality in sparse operations, but prior studies mostly focus on the case of sparse-matrix vector multiplication (SpMV).
items.abstract_zh: 稀疏矩阵-稀疏矩阵乘法 (SpGEMM) 是许多科学应用和图形工作负载中的关键内核。不幸的是，由于其不规则的内存访问模式，SpGEMM 受到数据移动的瓶颈。人们致力于开发行重新排序方案以改善稀疏运算中的局部性，但先前的研究主要集中在稀疏矩阵向量乘法（SpMV）的情况。

items.title: PerfDojo: Automated ML Library Generation for Heterogeneous Architectures.
items.abstract: The increasing complexity of machine learning models and the proliferation of diverse hardware architectures (CPUs, GPUs, accelerators) make achieving optimal performance a significant challenge. Heterogeneity in instruction sets, specialized kernel requirements for different data types and model features (e.g., sparsity, quantization), and architecture-specific optimizations complicate performance tuning. Manual optimization is resource-intensive, while existing automatic approaches often rely on complex hardware-specific heuristics and uninterpretable intermediate representations, hindering performance portability. We introduce PerfLLM, a novel automatic optimization methodology leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs) and Reinforcement Learning (RL). Central to this is PerfDojo, an environment framing optimization as an RL game using a human-readable, mathematically-inspired code representation that guarantees semantic validity through transformations. This allows effective optimization without prior hardware knowledge, facilitating both human analysis and RL agent training. We demonstrate PerfLLM’s ability to achieve significant performance gains across diverse CPU (x86, Arm, RISC-V) and GPU architectures.
items.abstract_zh: 机器学习模型的日益复杂性和多样化硬件架构（CPU、GPU、加速器）的激增使得实现最佳性能成为一项重大挑战。指令集的异构性、不同数据类型和模型特征（例如稀疏性、量化）的专门内核要求以及特定于体系结构的优化使性能调整变得复杂。手动优化是资源密集型的，而现有的自动方法通常依赖于复杂的特定于硬件的启发式方法和无法解释的中间表示，从而阻碍了性能的可移植性。我们介绍 PerfLLM，这是一种利用大型语言模型 (LLM) 和强化学习 (RL) 的新型自动优化方法。其中的核心是 PerfDojo，这是一种作为 RL 游戏的环境框架优化，使用人类可读的、受数学启发的代码表示形式，通过转换保证语义有效性。这样无需事先了解硬件知识即可进行有效优化，从而促进人工分析和 RL 代理训练。我们展示了 PerfLLM 在不同的 CPU（x86、Arm、RISC-V）和 GPU 架构中实现显着性能提升的能力。

items.title: SIREN: Software Identification and Recognition in HPC Systems.
items.abstract: HPC systems use monitoring and operational data analytics to ensure efficiency, performance, and orderly operations. Application-specific insights are crucial for analyzing the increasing complexity and diversity of HPC workloads, particularly through the identification of unknown software and recognition of repeated executions, which facilitate system optimization and security improvements. However, traditional identification methods using job or file names are unreliable for arbitrary user-provided names. Fuzzy hashing the content of executables detects similarities despite different code versions or compilation approaches while preserving privacy and file integrity, overcoming these limitations. We introduce SIREN, a process-level data collection framework for software identification and recognition. SIREN improves observability in HPC job execution by enabling analysis of process metadata, environment information, and executable fuzzy hashes. Findings from an opt-in deployment campaign on LUMI show SIREN’s ability to provide insights into software usage, recognition of repeated executions of known applications, and similarity-based identification of unknown applications.
items.abstract_zh: HPC 系统使用监控和运营数据分析来确保效率、性能和有序运营。针对特定应用程序的洞察对于分析 HPC 工作负载日益增加的复杂性和多样性至关重要，特别是通过识别未知软件和识别重复执行，从而促进系统优化和安全性改进。然而，使用作业或文件名的传统识别方法对于任意用户提供的名称来说并不可靠。尽管代码版本或编译方法不同，但模糊散列可执行文件的内容可以检测相似性，同时保留隐私和文件完整性，克服这些限制。我们介绍 SIREN，一个用于软件识别和识别的过程级数据收集框架。 SIREN 通过对进程元数据、环境信息和可执行模糊哈希进行分析，提高了 HPC 作业执行的可观察性。 LUMI 上的选择加入部署活动的结果表明，SIREN 能够深入了解软件使用情况、识别已知应用程序的重复执行以及基于相似性识别未知应用程序。

items.title: HELM: Characterizing Unified Memory Accesses to Improve GPU Performance under Memory Oversubscription.
items.abstract: Unified Memory (UM) technologies simplify memory management across CPU and GPU domains in GPU-accelerated heterogeneous architectures through transparent data migration. However, the default migration mechanism can severely degrade performance when applications oversubscribe GPU memory. Existing approaches to mitigating this performance degradation often fail to generalize, as they target specific application types, require specialized hardware, or integrate opaque classification methods.
items.abstract_zh: 统一内存 (UM) 技术通过透明的数据迁移，简化了 GPU 加速异构架构中跨 CPU 和 GPU 域的内存管理。然而，当应用程序过度订阅 GPU 内存时，默认迁移机制可能会严重降低性能。缓解这种性能下降的现有方法通常无法推广，因为它们针对特定的应用程序类型，需要专门的硬件或集成不透明的分类方法。

items.title: Scaling Out Chip Interconnect Networks with Implicit Sequence Numbers.
items.abstract: As AI models outpace the capabilities of single processors, interconnects across chips have become a critical enabler for scalable computing. These processors exchange massive amounts of data at cache-line granularity, prompting the adoption of new interconnect protocols like CXL, NVLink, and UALink, designed for high bandwidth and small payloads. However, the increasing transfer rates of these protocols heighten susceptibility to errors. While mechanisms like Cyclic Redundancy Check (CRC) and Forward Error Correction (FEC) are standard for reliable data transmission, scaling chip interconnects to multi-node configurations introduces new challenges, particularly in managing silently dropped flits in switching devices.
items.abstract_zh: 随着人工智能模型超越单个处理器的能力，跨芯片互连已成为可扩展计算的关键推动者。这些处理器以缓存行粒度交换大量数据，促使采用专为高带宽和小有效负载而设计的新互连协议，例如 CXL、NVLink 和 UALink。然而，这些协议不断提高的传输速率增加了出错的可能性。虽然循环冗余校验 (CRC) 和前向纠错 (FEC) 等机制是可靠数据传输的标准，但将芯片互连扩展到多节点配置会带来新的挑战，特别是在管理交换设备中静默丢弃的 flits 方面。

items.title: Core Hours and Carbon Credits: Incentivizing Sustainability in HPC.
items.abstract: Efforts to reduce the environmental impact of HPC often focus on resource providers, but choices made by users, e.g., concerning where to run, can be equally consequential. Here we present evidence that new accounting methods that charge users for energy used can incentivize significantly more efficient behavior. We first survey 300 HPC users and find that fewer than 30% are aware of their energy consumption, and that energy efficiency is a low priority concern. We then propose two new multi-resource accounting methods that charge for computations based on their energy consumption or carbon footprint, respectively. Finally, we conduct both simulation studies and a user study to evaluate the impact of these two methods on user behavior. We find that while only providing users feedback on their energy use had no impact on their behavior, associating energy with cost incentivized users to select more efficient resources, and use 40% less energy.
items.abstract_zh: 减少 HPC 对环境影响的努力通常集中在资源提供者身上，但用户做出的选择（例如在哪里运行）也可能同样重要。在这里，我们提供的证据表明，向用户收取能源费用的新会计方法可以激励显着更高效的行为。我们首先调查了 300 名 HPC 用户，发现只有不到 30% 的人了解自己的能源消耗，并且能源效率并不是最重要的问题。然后，我们提出了两种新的多资源核算方法，分别根据能源消耗或碳足迹进行计算收费。最后，我们进行模拟研究和用户研究，以评估这两种方法对用户行为的影响。我们发现，虽然仅向用户提供有关其能源使用情况的反馈对他们的行为没有影响，但将能源与成本激励用户联系起来，以选择更高效的资源，并减少 40% 的能源使用。

items.title: NNQS-SCI: Tackling Trillion-Dimensional Hilbert Space with Adaptive Neural Network Quantum States.
items.abstract: Neural Network Quantum States (NNQS) offer a powerful variational Monte Carlo (VMC) approach for quantum many-body problems, balancing polynomial scaling with high expressive power. However, scaling NNQS to large chemical systems faces challenges in preserving accuracy with exact energy and managing vast configurations efficiently. In this work, we introduce NNQS-SCI, a high-performance Selected Configuration Interaction (SCI) based NNQS method designed to overcome these limitations. NNQS-SCI employs highly parallelized Slater-Condon rules for fast local energy evaluations, avoiding accuracy loss, while its adaptive SCI engine dynamically manages billions of configurations without space explosion or arbitrary cutoffs that plague other NNQS-CI approaches. Optimized for extreme scalability via multi-level parallelism and memory compression, NNQS-SCI successfully simulates systems up to 152 spin orbitals, tackling Hilbert space dimensions exceeding 1014 and demonstrating significant advances in scale and efficiency. NNQS-SCI thus provides a robust and scalable path towards high-accuracy quantum chemistry on high-performance computing platforms.
items.abstract_zh: 神经网络量子态 (NNQS) 为量子多体问题提供了强大的变分蒙特卡罗 (VMC) 方法，平衡多项式缩放与高表达能力。然而，将 NNQS 扩展到大型化学系统面临着保持准确能量和有效管理大量配置的挑战。在这项工作中，我们介绍了 NNQS-SCI，这是一种基于高性能选择配置交互 (SCI) 的 NNQS 方法，旨在克服这些限制。 NNQS-SCI 采用高度并行的 Slater-Condon 规则进行快速局部能量评估，避免精度损失，同时其自适应 SCI 引擎动态管理数十亿个配置，而不会出现困扰其他 NNQS-CI 方法的空间爆炸或任意中断。 NNQS-SCI 通过多级并行性和内存压缩进行了极端可扩展性的优化，成功模拟了多达 152 个自旋轨道的系统，解决了超过 1014 个希尔伯特空间维度的问题，并展示了规模和效率方面的显着进步。因此，NNQS-SCI 为高性能计算平台上的高精度量子化学提供了一条稳健且可扩展的路径。

items.title: Scaling the memory wall using mixed-precision - HPG-MxP on an exascale machine.
items.abstract: Mixed-precision algorithms have been proposed as a way for scientific computing to benefit from some of the gains seen for artificial intelligence (AI) on recent high performance computing (HPC) platforms. A few applications dominated by dense matrix operations have seen substantial speedups by utilizing low precision formats such as FP16. However, a majority of scientific simulation applications are memory bandwidth limited. Beyond preliminary studies, the practical gain from using mixed-precision algorithms on a given HPC system is largely unclear.
items.abstract_zh: 混合精度算法已被提议作为科学计算从人工智能 (AI) 在最新高性能计算 (HPC) 平台上取得的一些成果中获益的一种方式。通过使用低精度格式（例如 FP16），一些以密集矩阵运算为主的应用程序已经获得了显着的加速。然而，大多数科学模拟应用程序的内存带宽都受到限制。除了初步研究之外，在给定 HPC 系统上使用混合精度算法的实际收益在很大程度上尚不清楚。

items.title: SDR-RDMA: Software-Defined Reliability Architecture for Planetary Scale RDMA Communication.
items.abstract: RDMA is vital for efficient distributed training across datacenters, but millisecond-scale latencies complicate the design of its reliability layer. We show that depending on long-haul link characteristics, such as drop rate, distance and bandwidth, the widely used Selective Repeat algorithm can be inefficient, warranting alternatives like Erasure Coding. To enable such alternatives on existing hardware, we propose SDR-RDMA, a software-defined reliability stack for RDMA. Its core is a lightweight SDR SDK that extends standard point-to-point RDMA semantics - fundamental to AI networking stacks - with a receive buffer bitmap. SDR bitmap enables partial message completion to let applications implement custom reliability schemes tailored to specific deployments, while preserving zero-copy RDMA benefits. By offloading the SDR backend to NVIDIA's Data Path Accelerator (DPA), we achieve line-rate performance, enabling efficient inter-datacenter communication and advancing reliability innovation for inter-datacenter training.
items.abstract_zh: RDMA 对于跨数据中心的高效分布式训练至关重要，但毫秒级的延迟使其可靠性层的设计变得复杂。我们表明，根据长距离链路特征，例如丢包率、距离和带宽，广泛使用的选择性重复算法可能效率低下，需要使用擦除编码等替代方案。为了在现有硬件上实现此类替代方案，我们提出了 SDR-RDMA，这是一种用于 RDMA 的软件定义的可靠性堆栈。其核心是一个轻量级 SDR SDK，它通过接收缓冲区位图扩展了标准点对点 RDMA 语义（AI 网络堆栈的基础）。 SDR 位图支持部分消息完成，让应用程序实现针对特定部署量身定制的自定义可靠性方案，同时保留零复制 RDMA 优势。通过将 SDR 后端卸载到 NVIDIA 的数据路径加速器 (DPA)，我们实现了线速性能，实现高效的数据中心间通信并推进数据中心间训练的可靠性创新。

items.title: Computing the Full Earth System at 1km Resolution.
items.abstract: We present the first-ever global simulation of the full Earth system at 1.25 km grid spacing, achieving highest time compression with an unseen number of degrees of freedom. Our model captures the flow of energy, water, and carbon through key components of the Earth system: atmosphere, ocean, and land. To achieve this landmark simulation, we harness the power of 8192 GPUs on Alps and 20480 GPUs on JUPITER, two of the world’s largest GH200 superchip installations. We use both the Grace CPUs and Hopper GPUs by carefully balancing Earth’s components in a heterogeneous setup and optimizing acceleration techniques available in ICON’s codebase. We show how separation of concerns can reduce the code complexity by half while increasing performance and portability. Our achieved time compression of 145.7 simulated days per day enables long studies including full interactions in the Earth system and even outperforms earlier atmosphere-only simulations at a similar resolution.
items.abstract_zh: 我们首次以 1.25 公里网格间距对整个地球系统进行全球模拟，以前所未有的自由度实现了最高的时间压缩。我们的模型捕捉了地球系统关键组成部分（大气、海洋和陆地）的能量、水和碳的流动。为了实现这一具有里程碑意义的模拟，我们利用了 Alps 上的 8192 个 GPU 和 JUPITER 上的 20480 个 GPU 的强大功能，这是世界上最大的两个 GH200 超级芯片安装。我们使用 Grace CPU 和 Hopper GPU，在异构设置中仔细平衡地球的组件，并优化 ICON 代码库中可用的加速技术。我们展示了关注点分离如何将代码复杂性降低一半，同时提高性能和可移植性。我们实现了每天 145.7 天模拟的时间压缩，能够进行长期研究，包括地球系统中的全面相互作用，甚至在类似分辨率下优于早期的仅大气模拟。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for SC25 Paper FaSTCC: Fast Sparse Tensor Contractions on CPUs.
items.abstract: This reproducibility report provides details about the artifact evaluation done with regards to the Artifact Description and Evaluation appendix of SC25 paper FaSTCC: Fast Sparse Tensor Contractions on CPUs by Hunter McCoy et al. The work was done as part of the Reproducibility Initiative of SC25. The author is a member of the SC25 Reproducibilty Committee.
items.abstract_zh: 该再现性报告提供了有关根据 Hunter McCoy 等人的 SC25 论文 FaSTCC：CPU 上的快速稀疏张量收缩的工件描述和评估附录进行的工件评估的详细信息。这项工作是作为 SC25 再现性倡议的一部分完成的。作者是 SC25 重现性委员会成员。

items.title: TT-LoRA MoE: Using Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning and Sparse Mixture-Of-Experts.
items.abstract: We propose Tensor-Trained Low-Rank Adaptation Mixture of Experts (TT-LoRA MoE), a novel computational framework integrating Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) with sparse MoE routing to address scalability challenges in large model deployments. Unlike traditional MoE approaches, which face substantial computational overhead as expert counts grow, TT-LoRA MoE decomposes training into two distinct, optimized stages. First, we independently train lightweight, tensorized low-rank adapters (TT-LoRA experts), each specialized for specific tasks. Subsequently, these expert adapters remain frozen, eliminating inter-task interference and catastrophic forgetting in multi-task setting. A sparse MoE router, trained separately, dynamically leverages base model representations to select exactly one specialized adapter per input at inference time, automating expert selection without explicit task specification. This structured decoupling significantly enhances computational efficiency and flexibility: uses only 2% of LoRA, 0.3% of Adapters and 0.03% of AdapterFusion parameters and outperforms AdapterFusion by 4 % on average in multi-tasking, enabling practical and scalable multi-task inference deployments.
items.abstract_zh: 我们提出了张量训练的低秩适应专家混合 (TT-LoRA MoE)，这是一种新颖的计算框架，它将参数高效微调 (PEFT) 与稀疏 MoE 路由相结合，以解决大型模型部署中的可扩展性挑战。传统的 MoE 方法随着专家数量的增长而面临大量的计算开销，而 TT-LoRA MoE 将训练分解为两个不同的优化阶段。首先，我们独立训练轻量级、张量化的低阶适配器（TT-LoRA 专家），每个适配器专门用于特定任务。随后，这些专家适配器保持冻结状态，消除了多任务设置中的任务间干扰和灾难性遗忘。单独训练的稀疏 MoE 路由器动态地利用基本模型表示，在推理时为每个输入精确选择一个专用适配器，从而自动进行专家选择，而无需明确的任务规范。这种结构化解耦显着提高了计算效率和灵活性：仅使用 2% 的 LoRA、0.3% 的 Adapters 和 0.03% 的 AdapterFusion 参数，在多任务处理中平均优于 AdapterFusion 4%，从而实现实用且可扩展的多任务推理部署。

items.title: Microscopic-Level Mouse Whole Cortex Simulation Composed of 9 Million Biophysical Neurons and 26 Billion Synapses on the Supercomputer Fugaku.
items.abstract: Understanding the brain remains a major scientific challenge due to its complex structure and function. Unlike artificial neural networks, the biological brain features diverse biophysical properties essential to its function. Building an accurate digital replica using extensive anatomical and physiological data, which are available in standardized public databases, has emerged as a promising approach. In this study, a light-weight biophysical neuron simulator was developed and optimized to the supercomputer Fugaku. Good strong scaling was demonstrated in a benchmark model upto 152,064 compute nodes with 7.13 petaflops performance. In a more realistic scenario, a whole cerebral cortex of a mouse, consisting of 9 million biophysical neurons and 26 billion synapses, was simulated on the full-scale Fugaku with 145,728 nodes. These suggest that the present high-performance computing technology is ready to support the construction of a digital replica of the whole mammalian brain.
items.abstract_zh: 由于其复杂的结构和功能，了解大脑仍然是一项重大的科学挑战。与人工神经网络不同，生物大脑具有对其功能至关重要的多种生物物理特性。使用标准化公共数据库中提供的大量解剖和生理数据构建准确的数字复制品已成为一种有前景的方法。在这项研究中，开发了一种轻量级生物物理神经元模拟器，并针对超级计算机 Fugaku 进行了优化。在高达 152,064 个计算节点和 7.13 petaflops 性能的基准模型中展示了良好的强大扩展性。在更真实的场景中，在具有 145,728 个节点的全尺寸 Fugaku 上模拟了由 900 万个生物物理神经元和 260 亿个突触组成的小鼠整个大脑皮层。这些表明，目前的高性能计算技术已经准备好支持构建整个哺乳动物大脑的数字复制品。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for SC25 Paper RAPTOR: Practical Numerical Profiling of Scientific Applications.
items.abstract: This reproducibility report provides details about the artifact evaluation done with regards to the Artifact Description and Evaluation appendix of SC25 paper RAPTOR: Practical Numerical Profiling of Scientific Applications by Hoerold et al. The work was done as part of the Reproducibility Initiative of SC25. The author is a member of the SC25 Reproducibility Committee.
items.abstract_zh: 该再现性报告提供了有关根据 Hoerold 等人的 SC25 论文《RAPTOR：科学应用的实用数值分析》的工件描述和评估附录进行的工件评估的详细信息。这项工作是作为 SC25 再现性倡议的一部分完成的。作者是 SC25 再现性委员会成员。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for SC25 Paper Addressing Reproducibility Challenges in HPC with Continuous Integration.
items.abstract: This reproducibility report provides details about the artifact evaluation done with regards to the Artifact Description and Evaluation appendix of SC25 paper Addressing Reproducibility Challenges in HPC with Continuous Integration by Hayot-Sasson et al. The work was done as part of the Reproducibility Initiative of SC25. The author is a member of the SC25 Reproducibility Committee.
items.abstract_zh: 此再现性报告提供了有关根据 Hayot-Sasson 等人撰写的 SC25 论文通过持续集成解决 HPC 再现性挑战的工件描述和评估附录所进行的工件评估的详细信息。这项工作是作为 SC25 再现性倡议的一部分完成的。作者是 SC25 再现性委员会成员。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for SC25 Paper Bine Trees: Enhancing Collective Operations by Optimizing Communication Locality.
items.abstract: This reproducibility report provides details about the artifact evaluation done with regards to the Artifact Description and Evaluation appendix of SC25 paper Bine Trees: Enhancing Collective Operations by Optimizing Communication Locality by Daniele De Sensi et al. The work was done as part of the Reproducibility Initiative of SC25. The author is a member of the SC25 Reproducibilty Committee.
items.abstract_zh: 该再现性报告提供了有关根据 Daniele De Sensi 等人撰写的 SC25 论文 Bine Trees: Enhancing Collective Operations by Optimizing Communication Locality 的工件描述和评估附录进行的工件评估的详细信息。这项工作是作为 SC25 再现性倡议的一部分完成的。作者是 SC25 重现性委员会成员。

items.title: Breaking the System Noise Barrier at Exascale.
items.abstract: To meet the increasing demands of parallel scientific applications, supercomputers continue to grow in both scale and complexity. The fastest supercomputer in the world, El Capitan, features over a million CPU cores and tens of thousands of GPUs. Applications running on such large-scale systems are particularly susceptible to system noise or interference caused by the operating system (OS) and other services running on the same compute nodes as the application.
items.abstract_zh: 为了满足并行科学应用日益增长的需求，超级计算机的规模和复杂性不断增长。 El Capitan 是世界上最快的超级计算机，拥有超过 100 万个 CPU 内核和数万个 GPU。在此类大型系统上运行的应用程序特别容易受到操作系统 (OS) 和与应用程序在同一计算节点上运行的其他服务引起的系统噪声或干扰。

items.title: Fine-grained Automated Failure Management for Extreme-Scale GPU Accelerated Systems.
items.abstract: As high-performance computing (HPC) systems scale in size, system wide hardware failure rates increase. Historical data from previous large-scale HPC installations illustrate this trend, with the mean time between failures (MTBF) decreasing steadily over the past decade. Recent studies from artificial intelligence and machine-learning (AI/ML) training extrapolate MTBF declining even further for future GPU accelerated systems. As MTBF decreases, mean time to repair (MTTR) becomes more pronounced, highlighting the need for efficient recovery strategies.
items.abstract_zh: 随着高性能计算 (HPC) 系统规模的扩大，系统范围内的硬件故障率也会增加。之前大规模 HPC 安装的历史数据说明了这一趋势，平均故障间隔时间 (MTBF) 在过去十年中稳步下降。最近的人工智能和机器学习 (AI/ML) 训练研究推断，未来 GPU 加速系统的 MTBF 会进一步下降。随着 MTBF 减少，平均修复时间 (MTTR) 变得更加明显，凸显了高效恢复策略的必要性。

items.title: SparStencil: Retargeting Sparse Tensor Cores to Scientific Stencil Computations via Structured Sparsity Transformation.
items.abstract: Sparse Tensor Cores offer exceptional performance gains for AI workloads by exploiting structured 2:4 sparsity. However, their potential remains untapped for core scientific workloads such as stencil computations, which exhibit irregular sparsity patterns.
items.abstract_zh: 稀疏张量核心通过利用结构化 2:4 稀疏度为 AI 工作负载提供卓越的性能提升。然而，对于核心科学工作负载（例如表现出不规则稀疏模式的模板计算），它们的潜力尚未开发。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for SC25 Paper Sparsified Preconditioned Conjugate Gradient Solver on GPUs.
items.abstract: This reproducibility report provides details about the artifact evaluation done with regards to the Artifact Description and Evaluation appendix of SC25 paper Sparsified Preconditioned Conjugate Gradient Solver on GPUs by Hari Sundar et al. The work was done as part of the Reproducibility Initiative of SC25. The author is a member of the SC25 Reproducibility Committee.
items.abstract_zh: 该再现性报告提供了有关 Hari Sundar 等人根据 SC25 论文《GPU 上的稀疏预条件共轭梯度求解器》的工件描述和评估附录进行的工件评估的详细信息。这项工作是作为 SC25 再现性倡议的一部分完成的。作者是 SC25 再现性委员会成员。

items.title: Diff-MoE: Efficient Batched MoE Inference with Priority-Driven Differential Expert Caching.
items.abstract: The emerging Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) model mitigates the high compute cost of large-scale LLMs by sparsely activating a subset of experts during inference. However, MoE requires storing massive expert parameters, creating a severe memory bottleneck on resource-constrained GPUs. Existing approaches offload parameters to host memory and prefetch activated experts to GPU memory with sophisticated policies, but these solutions are tailored to single-batch inference and suffer from communication bottlenecks at larger batch sizes, limiting throughput. We identify two forms of locality in expert activation: a small set of experts are frequently invoked across inference (global locality), while others recur within short decoding bursts (temporal locality). To exploit this, we propose Diff-MoE, which introduces a differential cache hierarchy in GPU memory. Globally hot experts reside in per-layer high-priority caches, locally hot ones are dynamically managed in per-layer medium-priority caches under a priority-driven replacement policy, and the remaining cold experts are cached temporarily and evicted on demand. Moreover, Diff-MoE incorporates a lightweight predictor that prefetches experts likely needed in the next MoE layer, overlapping migration with computation to further reduce latency. Our evaluation shows that Diff-MoE improves inference throughput by 2.74 ×, 2.22 ×, and 1.55 × over DeepSpeed, Pre-gated MoE, and MoE-Infinity, respectively.
items.abstract_zh: 新兴的专家混合 (MoE) 模型通过在推理过程中稀疏地激活专家子集来降低大规模法学硕士的高计算成本。然而，MoE 需要存储大量专家参数，这对资源有限的 GPU 造成了严重的内存瓶颈。现有方法将参数卸载到主机内存，并使用复杂的策略将激活的专家预取到 GPU 内存，但这些解决方案是针对单批次推理而定制的，并且在较大批次大小时会遇到通信瓶颈，从而限制了吞吐量。我们确定了专​​家激活中的两种形式的局部性：一小组专家在推理过程中经常被调用（全局局部性），而其他专家则在短解码突发中重复出现（时间局部性）。为了利用这一点，我们提出了 Diff-MoE，它在 GPU 内存中引入了差异缓存层次结构。全局热专家驻留在每层高优先级缓存中，局部热专家根据优先级驱动的替换策略在每层中优先级缓存中动态管理，其余冷专家临时缓存并按需驱逐。此外，Diff-MoE 还包含一个轻量级预测器，可以预取下一个 MoE 层可能需要的专家，将迁移与计算重叠，以进一步减少延迟。我们的评估表明，与 DeepSpeed、Pre-gate MoE 和 MoE-Infinity 相比，Diff-MoE 的推理吞吐量分别提高了 2.74 ×、2.22 × 和 1.55 ×。

items.title: HP-MDR: High-performance and Portable Data Refactoring and Progressive Retrieval with Advanced GPUs.
items.abstract: Scientific applications produce vast amounts of data, posing grand challenges in the underlying data management and analytic tasks. Progressive compression is a promising way to address this problem, as it allows for on-demand data retrieval with significantly reduced data movement cost. However, most existing progressive methods are designed for CPUs, leaving a gap for them to unleash the power of today’s heterogeneous computing systems with GPUs.In this work, we propose HP-MDR, a high-performance and portable data refactoring and progressive retrieval framework for GPUs. Our contributions are four-fold: (1) We carefully optimize the bitplane encoding and lossless encoding, two key stages in progressive methods, to achieve high performance on GPUs; (2) We propose pipeline optimization and incorporate it with data refactoring and progressive retrieval workflows to further enhance the performance for large data process; (3) We leverage our framework to enable high-performance data retrieval with guaranteed error control for common Quantities of Interest; (4) We evaluate HP-MDR and compare it with state of the arts using five real-world datasets. Experimental results demonstrate that HP-MDR delivers an average 13.68 × and 6.31 × throughput in data refactoring and progressive retrieval tasks, respectively. It also leads to 11.22 × throughput for recomposing required data representations under Quantity-of-Interest error control and 6.04 × performance for the corresponding end-to-end data retrieval, when compared with state-of-the-art solutions.
items.abstract_zh: 科学应用程序会产生大量数据，给底层数据管理和分析任务带来巨大挑战。渐进式压缩是解决此问题的一种有前途的方法，因为它允许按需数据检索，并显着降低数据移动成本。然而，大多数现有的渐进方法都是为 CPU 设计的，这为它们利用 GPU 释放当今异构计算系统的能力留下了差距。在这项工作中，我们提出了 HP-MDR，一种用于 GPU 的高性能、可移植的数据重构和渐进检索框架。我们的贡献有四个方面：（1）我们仔细优化了位平面编码和无损编码（渐进式方法中的两个关键阶段），以在 GPU 上实现高性能； （2）我们提出管道优化，并将其与数据重构和渐进检索工作流程相结合，以进一步提高大数据处理的性能； (3) 我们利用我们的框架来实现高性能数据检索，并保证对常见感兴趣数量的错误控制； (4) 我们评估 HP-MDR 并使用五个真实数据集将其与现有技术进行比较。实验结果表明，HP-MDR 在数据重构和渐进式检索任务中的平均吞吐量分别为 13.68 倍和 6.31 倍。与最先进的解决方案相比，在兴趣数量错误控制下重构所需数据表示的吞吐量提高了 11.22 倍，相应的端到端数据检索的性能提高了 6.04 倍。

items.title: SlimPipe: Memory-Thrifty and Efficient Pipeline Parallelism for Long-Context LLM Training.
items.abstract: Pipeline Parallelism serves as a crucial technique for training Large Language Models, as it alleviates memory pressure from model states with relatively low communication overhead. However, in long-context scenarios, existing pipeline parallelism methods fail to address the substantial activation memory pressure, primarily due to the peak memory consumption resulting from the accumulation of activations across multiple microbatches. Moreover, these approaches inevitably introduce considerable pipeline bubbles, further hindering efficiency.
items.abstract_zh: 管道并行是训练大型语言模型的关键技术，因为它以相对较低的通信开销减轻了模型状态的内存压力。然而，在长上下文场景中，现有的管道并行方法无法解决巨大的激活内存压力，这主要是由于跨多个微批次的激活累积导致的峰值内存消耗。此外，这些方法不可避免地会引入相当大的管道气泡，进一步阻碍效率。

items.title: MISA-AKMC : Achieve Kinetic Monte Carlo Simulation of 20 Quadrillion Atoms on GPU Clusters.
items.abstract: The Atomic Kinetic Monte Carlo (AKMC) method provides insights into the macroscopic behavior of materials through atomistic-level simulations and finds broad applications in materials science innovation. Improving simulation scale and performance remains a consistent focus in the development of parallel AKMC software. We port the AKMC software to GPU clusters. To alleviate the memory pressure in large-scale complex system simulations, we redesign the data layout and propose the Lattice Data Compression and Vacancy Data Decompression algorithms. Additionally, We propose a multi-level pipeline scheme combined with an on-demand communication forwarding and merging strategy to reduce data transfer and communication overhead. Compared to state-of-the-art KMC software, MISA-AKMC achieves a 10.41-fold improvement in computational throughput and a 52.07-fold expansion in simulation scale. We implement the first true micrometer-scale AKMC simulation involving 20 quadrillion atoms on GPU clusters. MISA-AKMC achieves 96.03% parallel efficiency in weak scaling and 85.29% in strong scaling on 16,000 GPUs.
items.abstract_zh: 原子动力学蒙特卡罗 (AKMC) 方法通过原子级模拟提供对材料宏观行为的洞察，并在材料科学创新中得到广泛应用。提高仿真规模和性能仍然是并行 AKMC 软件开发的一贯重点。我们将 AKMC 软件移植到 GPU 集群。为了缓解大规模复杂系统仿真中的内存压力，我们重新设计了数据布局，并提出了格子数据压缩和空位数据解压缩算法。此外，我们提出了一种多级管道方案，结合按需通信转发和合并策略，以减少数据传输和通信开销。与最先进的KMC软件相比，MISA-AKMC的计算吞吐量提高了10.41倍，仿真规模扩大了52.07倍。我们在 GPU 集群上实现了第一个真正的微米级 AKMC 模拟，涉及 20 个万亿个原子。 MISA-AKMC 在 16,000 个 GPU 上，弱扩展时的并行效率为 96.03%，强扩展时的并行效率为 85.29%。

items.title: Optimizing Data Acquisitions in Multi-Robot Systems.
items.abstract: We present ROSfs, a novel user-level file system designed to address critical data query inefficiencies in multi-robot systems (MRS). ROSfs introduces an innovative file organization model where robot data is structured as labeled sub-files, coupled with a time-indexed architecture that enables efficient querying of actively modified data. This design enables real-time cross-robot data acquisition and collaboration capabilities previously unattainable in MRS deployments. Our implementation integrates seamlessly with the Robot Operating System (ROS) and has been extensively evaluated using both physical UAV/UGV platforms and data servers. Experimental results demonstrate that ROSfs achieves a 7x reduction in online data query latency under wireless network conditions compared to conventional ROS storage methods, while simultaneously improving data freshness (Age of Information) by up to 271x. These advancements position ROSfs as a transformative solution for high-performance robotic data management in distributed systems.
items.abstract_zh: 我们推出了 ROSfs，这是一种新颖的用户级文件系统，旨在解决多机器人系统 (MRS) 中关键数据查询效率低下的问题。 ROSfs 引入了一种创新的文件组织模型，其中机器人数据被构造为带标签的子文件，再加上时间索引架构，可以有效查询主动修改的数据。这种设计实现了实时跨机器人数据采集和协作功能，这是以前在 MRS 部署中无法实现的。我们的实施与机器人操作系统 (ROS) 无缝集成，并已使用物理 UAV/UGV 平台和数据服务器进行了广泛评估。实验结果表明，与传统ROS存储方法相比，ROSfs在无线网络条件下实现了在线数据查询延迟降低7倍，同时将数据新鲜度（信息年龄）提高高达271倍。这些进步使 ROSfs 成为分布式系统中高性能机器人数据管理的变革性解决方案。

items.title: Generative Latent Diffusion for Efficient Spatiotemporal Data Reduction.
items.abstract: Generative models have demonstrated strong performance in conditional settings and can be viewed as a form of data compression, where the condition serves as a compact representation. However, their limited controllability and reconstruction accuracy restrict their practical application to data compression. In this work, we propose an efficient latent diffusion framework that bridges this gap by combining a variational autoencoder with a conditional diffusion model. Our method compresses only a small number of keyframes into latent space and uses them as conditioning inputs to reconstruct the remaining frames via generative interpolation, eliminating the need to store latent representations for every frame. This approach enables accurate spatiotemporal reconstruction while significantly reducing storage costs. Experimental results across multiple datasets show that our method achieves up to 10× higher compression ratios than rule-based state-of-the-art compressors such as SZ3, and up to 63% better performance than leading learning-based methods under the same reconstruction error.
items.abstract_zh: 生成模型在条件设置中表现出了强大的性能，并且可以被视为数据压缩的一种形式，其中条件作为紧凑的表示。然而，它们有限的可控性和重构精度限制了它们在数据压缩中的实际应用。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种有效的潜在扩散框架，通过将变分自动编码器与条件扩散模型相结合来弥补这一差距。我们的方法仅将少量关键帧压缩到潜在空间中，并使用它们作为条件输入，通过生成插值重建剩余帧，从而无需存储每个帧的潜在表示。这种方法可以实现准确的时空重建，同时显着降低存储成本。跨多个数据集的实验结果表明，我们的方法比基于规则的最先进压缩器（例如 SZ3）实现了高达 10 倍的压缩比，并且在相同的重建误差下比领先的基于学习的方法提高了高达 63% 的性能。

items.title: MaverIQ: Fingerprint-Guided Extrapolation and Fragmentation-Aware Layering for Intent-Based LLM Serving.
items.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are becoming ubiquitous across industries, where applications demand they fulfill diverse user intents. However, developers currently face the challenge of manually exploring numerous deployment configurations—combinations of parallelism and compression techniques that impact resource usage, latency, cost, and accuracy—to meet these intents. Previous works automate configuration selection and deployment, but, they (a) rely on expensive profiling, and (b) suboptimally utilize the fragmented resource availability in multi-tenant GPU clusters, inflating operational costs for the provider. Moreover, none of these solutions tailors deployment configuration decisions to diverse user intents.
items.abstract_zh: 大型语言模型 (LLM) 在各个行业中变得无处不在，这些应用程序要求它们满足不同的用户意图。然而，开发人员目前面临着手动探索众多部署配置（影响资源使用、延迟、成本和准确性的并行性和压缩技术的组合）以满足这些意图的挑战。以前的工作自动化了配置选择和部署，但是，它们 (a) 依赖于昂贵的分析，(b) 未充分利用多租户 GPU 集群中的碎片资源可用性，从而增加了提供商的运营成本。此外，这些解决方案都没有根据不同的用户意图定制部署配置决策。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for SC25 Paper XaaS Containers: Performance-Portable Representation With Source and IR Containers.
items.abstract: This reproducibility report provides details about the artifact evaluation done with regards to the Artifact Description and Evaluation appendix of SC25 paper XaaS Containers: Performance-Portable Representation With Source and IR Containers by Marcin Copik et al. The work was done as part of the Reproducibility Initiative of SC25. The author is a member of the SC25 Reproducibilty Committee.
items.abstract_zh: 此再现性报告提供了有关根据 Marcin Copik 等人的 SC25 论文《XaaS Containers：Performance-Portable Representation With Source and IR Containers》的工件描述和评估附录进行的工件评估的详细信息。这项工作是作为 SC25 再现性倡议的一部分完成的。作者是 SC25 重现性委员会成员。

items.title: COSMOS: Performance Portable Graph Pattern Matching with Domain-Specific Software Distributed Shared Memory.
items.abstract: Graph pattern matching (GPM) is essential in fields like circuit logic synthesis, anomaly detection, social network analysis, cheminformatics, recommendation systems, and classification systems. Its NP-completeness and the irregular nature of graph data make scaling to distributed systems challenging, especially for complex supercomputers. Although utilizing architecture-specific optimization can improve the performance of Graph Pattern Matching on large-scale data, such ad-hoc solution lacks performance portability that not only causes vendor lock-in but also complicates the parallel evolution of GPM software with hardware architectures. This paper proposes Cosmos, a domain-specific software distributed shared memory model (DSM) that shields diversity of supercomputers from users and developers, achieving both performance portability and performance. This approach enables the same code scaling to thousands of nodes across different supercomputers while maintaining performance comparable to manually optimized versions.
items.abstract_zh: 图模式匹配（GPM）在电路逻辑综合、异常检测、社交网络分析、化学信息学、推荐系统和分类系统等领域至关重要。它的 NP 完整性和图数据的不规则性质使得扩展到分布式系统具有挑战性，特别是对于复杂的超级计算机。虽然利用特定于架构的优化可以提高大规模数据上图模式匹配的性能，但这种临时解决方案缺乏性能可移植性，不仅导致供应商锁定，而且使 GPM 软件与硬件架构的并行演进变得复杂。本文提出了 Cosmos，一种特定领域的软件分布式共享内存模型（DSM），它可以屏蔽用户和开发人员的超级计算机的多样性，实现性能可移植性和性能。这种方法可以将相同的代码扩展到不同超级计算机上的数千个节点，同时保持与手动优化版本相当的性能。

items.title: DHAP: Towards Efficient OLAP in a Disaggregated and Heterogeneous Environment.
items.abstract: Disaggregation of hardware resources and integration of heterogeneous accelerators are two emerging trends in datacenters. Existing data systems focus on either disaggregated systems with homogeneous CPU processors or incorporation of heterogeneous accelerators within traditional monolithic servers. None can adequately address the challenges posed by systems that are both disaggregated and heterogeneous.
items.abstract_zh: 硬件资源的分解和异构加速器的集成是数据中心的两个新兴趋势。现有的数据系统要么侧重于具有同质 CPU 处理器的分解系统，要么侧重于在传统单片服务器中整合异构加速器。没有人能够充分解决分散且异构的系统带来的挑战。

items.title: Sparsified Preconditioned Conjugate Gradient Solver on GPUs.
items.abstract: Preconditioned iterative sparse linear solvers are memory-efficient for large scientific simulations, but the dependences between iterations introduced by preconditioners limit parallelization. This issue is exacerbated on GPUs, which feature many parallel cores. We propose a sparsified preconditioned conjugate gradient (SPCG) solver that increases parallelism by reducing dependences through sparsification, while preserving convergence behavior. We evaluate the proposed SPCG using both ILU(0) and ILU(K) preconditioners on a wide range of symmetric positive definite (SPD) matrices. The proposed SPCG improves the performance of the iterative phase of SPCG by a geometric mean speedup of 1.23 × and 1.65 × over the non-sparsified PCG using ILU(0) and ILU(K), respectively on an NVIDIA A100 GPU. SPCG also yields geometric mean end-to-end speedups of 1.68 × and 3.73 × over the non-sparsified versions with ILU(0) and ILU(K), respectively, on the same platform.
items.abstract_zh: 预处理迭代稀疏线性求解器对于大型科学模拟来说具有内存效率，但预处理器引入的迭代之间的依赖性限制了并行化。这个问题在具有许多并行核心的 GPU 上更加严重。我们提出了一种稀疏预条件共轭梯度（SPCG）求解器，它通过稀疏化减少依赖性来增加并行性，同时保留收敛行为。我们在各种对称正定 (SPD) 矩阵上使用 ILU(0) 和 ILU(K) 预处理器来评估所提出的 SPCG。与在 NVIDIA A100 GPU 上使用 ILU(0) 和 ILU(K) 的非稀疏 PCG 相比，所提出的 SPCG 分别将 SPCG 迭代阶段的性能提高了 1.23 × 和 1.65 × 几何平均加速。在同一平台上，与具有 ILU(0) 和 ILU(K) 的非稀疏版本相比，SPCG 的几何平均端到端加速分别为 1.68 × 和 3.73 ×。

items.title: mLR: Scalable Laminography Reconstruction based on Memoization.
items.abstract: ADMM-FFT is an iterative method with high reconstruction accuracy for laminography but suffers from excessive computation time and large memory consumption. We introduce mLR, which employs memoization to replace the time-consuming Fast Fourier Transform (FFT) operations based on an unique observation that similar FFT operations appear in iterations of ADMM-FFT. We introduce a series of techniques to make the application of memoization to ADMM-FFT performance-beneficial and scalable. We also introduce variable offloading to save CPU memory and scale ADMM-FFT across GPUs within and across nodes. Using mLR, we are able to scale ADMM-FFT on an input problem of 2K × 2K × 2K, which is the largest input problem laminography reconstruction has ever worked on with the ADMM-FFT solution on limited memory; mLR brings 52.8% performance improvement on average (up to 65.4%), compared to the original ADMM-FFT.
items.abstract_zh: ADMM-FFT是一种层层摄影重建精度高的迭代方法，但存在计算时间过长和内存消耗大的问题。我们引入了 mLR，它基于 ADMM-FFT 迭代中出现类似 FFT 操作的独特观察，采用记忆化来代替耗时的快速傅立叶变换 (FFT) 操作。我们引入了一系列技术，使记忆化在 ADMM-FFT 中的应用具有有益的性能和可扩展性。我们还引入变量卸载来节省 CPU 内存并在节点内和跨节点的 GPU 上扩展 ADMM-FFT。使用 mLR，我们能够在 2K × 2K × 2K 的输入问题上扩展 ADMM-FFT，这是在有限内存上使用 ADMM-FFT 解决方案处理过的最大输入问题层析成像重建；与原始 ADMM-FFT 相比，MLR 平均带来 52.8% 的性能提升（高达 65.4%）。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for SC25 Paper Optimizing Quantum Circuit Mapping to Reduce Inter-Module Communications in Distributed Architectures.
items.abstract: This reproducibility report provides details about the artifact evaluation done with regards to the Artifact Description and Evaluation appendix of SC25 paper "Optimizing Quantum Circuit Mapping to Reduce Inter-Module Communications in Distributed Architectures" by Xiulin Cui et al. The work was done as part of the Reproducibility Initiative of SC25. The author is a member of the SC25 Reproducibilty Committee.
items.abstract_zh: 该再现性报告提供了有关根据 Xiulin Cui 等人撰写的 SC25 论文“优化量子电路映射以减少分布式架构中的模块间通信”的工件描述和评估附录进行的工件评估的详细信息。这项工作是作为 SC25 再现性倡议的一部分完成的。作者是 SC25 重现性委员会成员。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for SC25 Paper C.A.T.S.: Memory and Control Flow Tracing for Whole-Program Performance Analysis.
items.abstract: This reproducibility report provides details about the artifact evaluation done with regards to the Artifact Description and Evaluation appendix of SC25 paper "C.A.T.S.: Memory and Control Flow Tracing for Whole-Program Performance Analysis" by Tal Ben-Nun et al. The work was done as part of the Reproducibility Initiative of SC25. The author is a member of the SC25 Reproducibilty Committee.
items.abstract_zh: 此再现性报告提供了有关根据 Tal Ben-Nun 等人撰写的 SC25 论文“C.A.T.S.：用于整个程序性能分析的内存和控制流跟踪”的工件描述和评估附录进行的工件评估的详细信息。这项工作是作为 SC25 再现性倡议的一部分完成的。作者是 SC25 重现性委员会成员。

items.title: MLP-Offload: Multi-Level, Multi-Path Offloading for LLM Pre-training to Break the GPU Memory Wall.
items.abstract: Training LLMs larger than the aggregated memory of multiple GPUs is increasingly necessary due to the faster growth of LLM sizes compared to GPU memory. To this end, multi-tier host memory or disk offloading techniques are proposed by state of art. Despite advanced asynchronous multi-tier read/write strategies, such offloading strategies result in significant I/O overheads in the critical path of training, resulting in slower iterations. To this end, we propose MLP-Offload, a novel multi-level, multi-path offloading engine specifically designed for optimizing LLM training on resource-constrained setups by mitigating I/O bottlenecks. We make several key observations that drive the design of MLP-Offload, such as I/O overheads during the update dominate the iteration time; I/O bandwidth of the third-level remote storage tier remains unutilized; and, contention due to concurrent offloading amplifies I/O bottlenecks. Driven by these insights, we design and implement MLP-Offload to offload the optimizer states across multiple tiers in a cache-efficient and concurrency-controlled fashion to mitigate I/O bottlenecks during the backward and update phases. Evaluations on models up to 280B parameters shows that MLP-Offload achieves 2.5 × faster iterations compared to the state-of-the-art LLM training runtimes.
items.abstract_zh: 由于与 GPU 内存相比，LLM 大小增长更快，因此训练大于多个 GPU 聚合内存的 LLM 变得越来越有必要。为此，现有技术提出了多层主机存储器或磁盘卸载技术。尽管有先进的异步多层读/写策略，但此类卸载策略会在训练的关键路径中产生大量 I/O 开销，从而导致迭代速度变慢。为此，我们提出了 MLP-Offload，这是一种新颖的多级、多路径卸载引擎，专门设计用于通过减轻 I/O 瓶颈来优化资源受限设置上的 LLM 训练。我们进行了几个推动 MLP-Offload 设计的关键观察，例如更新期间的 I/O 开销主导了迭代时间；第三级远程存储层的I/O带宽仍未得到利用；而且，并发卸载引起的争用会加剧 I/O 瓶颈。在这些见解的驱动下，我们设计并实现了 MLP-Offload，以缓存高效且并发控制的方式跨多个层卸载优化器状态，​​从而缓解向后和更新阶段的 I/O 瓶颈。对高达 280B 参数的模型的评估表明，与最先进的 LLM 训练运行时相比，MLP-Offload 的迭代速度提高了 2.5 倍。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for SC25 Paper SIGMo: High-Throughput Batched Subgraph Isomorphism on GPUs for Molecular Matching.
items.abstract: This reproducibility report provides details about the artifact evaluation done with regards to the Artifact Description and Evaluation appendix of SC25 paper SIGMo: High-Throughput Batched Subgraph Isomorphism on GPUs for Molecular Matching by Gennaro Cordasco et al. The work was done as part of the Reproducibility Initiative of SC25. The author is a member of the SC25 Reproducibilty Committee.
items.abstract_zh: 该再现性报告提供了有关根据 SC25 论文 SIGMo 的工件描述和评估附录进行的工件评估的详细信息：Gennaro Cordasco 等人的 GPU 上用于分子匹配的高吞吐量批量子图同构。这项工作是作为 SC25 再现性倡议的一部分完成的。作者是 SC25 重现性委员会成员。

items.title: Hetis: Serving LLMs in Heterogeneous GPU Clusters with Fine-grained and Dynamic Parallelism.
items.abstract: The significant resource demands in LLM serving prompts production clusters to fully utilize heterogeneous hardware by partitioning LLM models across a mix of high-end and low-end GPUs. However, existing parallelization approaches often struggle to scale efficiently in heterogeneous environments due to their coarse-grained and static parallelization strategies.
items.abstract_zh: LLM 服务中的大量资源需求促使生产集群通过在高端和低端 GPU 的组合上划分 LLM 模型来充分利用异构硬件。然而，现有的并行化方法由于其粗粒度和静态并行化策略，通常难以在异构环境中有效扩展。

items.title: PGT-I: Scaling Spatiotemporal GNNs with Memory-Efficient Distributed Training.
items.abstract: Spatiotemporal graph neural networks (ST-GNNs) are powerful tools for modeling spatial and temporal data dependencies. However, their applications have been limited primarily to small-scale datasets because of memory constraints. While distributed training offers a solution, current frameworks lack support for spatiotemporal models and overlook the properties of spatiotemporal data. Informed by a scaling study on a large-scale workload, we present PyTorch Geometric Temporal Index (PGT-I), an extension to PyTorch Geometric Temporal that integrates distributed data parallel training and two novel strategies: index-batching and distributed-index-batching. Our index techniques exploit spatiotemporal structure to construct snapshots dynamically at runtime, significantly reducing memory overhead, while distributed-index-batching extends this approach by enabling scalable processing across multiple GPUs. Our techniques enable the first-ever training of an ST-GNN on the entire PeMS dataset without graph partitioning, reducing peak memory usage by up to 89% and achieving up to a 11.78x speedup over standard DDP with 128 GPUs.
items.abstract_zh: 时空图神经网络（ST-GNN）是用于建模空间和时间数据依赖性的强大工具。然而，由于内存限制，它们的应用主要限于小规模数据集。虽然分布式训练提供了一种解决方案，但当前的框架缺乏对时空模型的支持，并且忽视了时空数据的属性。根据大规模工作负载的扩展研究，我们提出了 PyTorch Geometric Temporal Index (PGT-I)，它是 PyTorch Geometric Temporal 的扩展，集成了分布式数据并行训练和两种新颖的策略：索引批处理和分布式索引批处理。我们的索引技术利用时空结构在运行时动态构建快照，显着减少内存开销，而分布式索引批处理通过支持跨多个 GPU 的可扩展处理扩展了这种方法。我们的技术首次在整个 PeMS 数据集上训练 ST-GNN，无需进行图形分区，从而将峰值内存使用量减少高达 89%，并比使用 128 个 GPU 的标准 DDP 实现高达 11.78 倍的加速。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for SC25 Paper MetoHash: A Memory-Efficient and Traffic-Optimized Hashing Index on Hybrid PMem-DRAM Memories.
items.abstract: This reproducibility report provides details about the artifact evaluation done with regards to the Artifact Description and Evaluation appendix of SC25 paper MetoHash: A Memory-Efficient and Traffic-Optimized Hashing Index on Hybrid PMem-DRAM Memories by Xiaoli Wang, Ronglong Wu, Zixiang Yu, Zhirong Shen, Zhifeng Bao, Chuanhui Yang, Guangyang Deng, Quanqing Xu, and Qiangsheng Su. The work was done as part of the Reproducibility Initiative of SC25. The author is a member of the SC25 Reproducibility Committee.
items.abstract_zh: 该可重复性报告提供了有关 SC25 论文 MetoHash：混合 PMem-DRAM 内存上的内存高效和流量优化哈希索引（作者：Xiaoli Wang、Ronglong Wu、Zixiang Yu、Zhirong Shen、Zhifeng Bao、Chuanhui Yang、Guangyang Deng、Quanqing Xu 和 Qigsheng Su）的工件描述和评估附录的工件评估的详细信息。这项工作是作为 SC25 再现性倡议的一部分完成的。作者是 SC25 再现性委员会成员。

items.title: TENSORMD: Accelerating Molecular Dynamics with a High-Performance Machine Learning Interatomic Potential.
items.abstract: AI has been integrated into HPC across various scientific fields, significantly enhancing performance. In molecular dynamics simulations, HPC+AI facilitates the investigation of atomic-scale physical properties using machine-learning interatomic potentials (MLIPs). However, general-purpose ML tools (e.g., TensorFlow) used in MLIPs are not optimally matched, leading to missed optimization opportunities due to the higher computational complexity and greater diversity of HPC+AI applications compared to pure AI scenarios. To address this, we introduce TensorMD, an MLIP independent of existing ML tools, enabling flexible optimizations that standard ML frameworks cannot support. TensorMD outperforms a state-of-the-art MLIP—winner of the 2020 Gordon Bell Prize and built on an ML tool—by 1.88 × on NVIDIA A100 GPU. Additionally, TensorMD was evaluated on two supercomputers with different architectures, achieving significantly reduced time-to-solution and supporting molecular dynamics simulations at scales beyond 50 billion atoms.
items.abstract_zh: 人工智能已被集成到各个科学领域的高性能计算中，显着提高了性能。在分子动力学模拟中，HPC+AI 有助于利用机器学习原子间势 (MLIP) 来研究原子尺度的物理性质。然而，MLIP 中使用的通用机器学习工具（例如 TensorFlow）并未得到最佳匹配，由于与纯 AI 场景相比，HPC+AI 应用的计算复杂度更高且多样性更大，导致错失优化机会。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了 TensorMD，这是一种独立于现有 ML 工具的 MLIP，可以实现标准 ML 框架无法支持的灵活优化。 TensorMD 在 NVIDIA A100 GPU 上的性能比基于 ML 工具构建的最先进的 MLIP（2020 年戈登贝尔奖得主）高出 1.88 倍。此外，TensorMD 在两台具有不同架构的超级计算机上进行了评估，显着缩短了求解时间，并支持超过 500 亿个原子规模的分子动力学模拟。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for SC25 Paper TurboFNO: High-Performance Fourier Neural Operator with Fused FFT-GEMM-iFFT on GPU.

items.title: Augmenting Simulated Noisy Quantum Data Collection by Orders of Magnitude Using Pre-Trajectory Sampling with Batched Execution.
items.abstract: Classically simulating quantum systems is challenging, as even noiseless n-qubit quantum states scale as 2n. The complexity of noisy quantum systems is even greater, requiring 2n × 2n-dimensional density matrices. Various approximations reduce density matrix overhead, including quantum trajectory-based methods, which instead use an ensemble of m ≪ 2n noisy states. While this method is dramatically more efficient, current implementations use unoptimized sampling, redundant state preparation, and single-shot data collection. In this manuscript, we present the Pre-Trajectory Sampling technique, increasing the efficiency and utility of trajectory simulations by tailoring error types, batching sampling without redundant computation, and collecting error information. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our method with both a mature statevector simulation of a 35-qubit quantum error-correction code and a preliminary tensor network simulation of 85 qubits, yielding speedups of up to 106x and 16x, as well as generating massive datasets of one trillion and one million shots, respectively.
items.abstract_zh: 经典模拟量子系统具有挑战性，因为即使是无噪声的 n 量子位量子态规模也可达 2n。有噪声的量子系统的复杂性甚至更大，需要 2n × 2n 维密度矩阵。各种近似减少了密度矩阵开销，包括基于量子轨迹的方法，该方法使用 m < 2n 个噪声状态的集合。虽然这种方法的效率显着提高，但当前的实现使用未优化的采样、冗余状态准备和单次数据收集。在这篇手稿中，我们提出了预轨迹采样技术，通过定制错误类型、无需冗余计算的批量采样以及收集错误信息来提高轨迹模拟的效率和实用性。我们通过 35 量子位量子纠错码的成熟状态向量模拟和 85 量子位的初步张量网络模拟来证明我们方法的有效性，分别产生高达 106 倍和 16 倍的加速，并分别生成一万亿和一百万次的海量数据集。

items.title: Parallel Rank-Adaptive Higher Order Orthogonal Iteration.
items.abstract: Higher Order Orthogonal Iteration (HOOI) is an iterative algorithm that computes a Tucker decomposition of fixed ranks of an input tensor. In this work we modify HOOI to determine ranks adaptively subject to a fixed approximation error, apply optimizations to reduce the cost of each HOOI iteration, and parallelize the method in order to scale to large dense datasets. We show that HOOI is competitive with the Sequentially Truncated Higher Order Singular Value Decomposition (STHOSVD) algorithm, particularly in cases of high compression ratios. Our proposed rank-adaptive HOOI can achieve comparable approximation error to STHOSVD in less time, sometimes achieving a better compression ratio. We demonstrate that our parallelization scales well over thousands of cores and show using three scientific simulation datasets that HOOI outperforms STHOSVD in high-compression regimes. For example, for a 3D fluid-flow simulation dataset, HOOI computed a Tucker decomposition 82x faster and achieved a compression ratio 50% better than STHOSVD’s.
items.abstract_zh: 高阶正交迭代 (HOOI) 是一种迭代算法，用于计算输入张量固定秩的塔克分解。在这项工作中，我们修改 HOOI 来自适应地确定受固定近似误差影响的排名，应用优化来降低每次 HOOI 迭代的成本，并对方法进行并行化，以便扩展到大型密集数据集。我们表明，HOOI 与顺序截断高阶奇异值分解 (STHOSVD) 算法相比具有竞争力，特别是在高压缩比的情况下。我们提出的等级自适应 HOOI 可以在更短的时间内实现与 STHOSVD 相当的近似误差，有时还可以实现更好的压缩比。我们证明了我们的并行化可以扩展到数千个核心，并使用三个科学模拟数据集证明 HOOI 在高压缩状态下的性能优于 STHOSVD。例如，对于 3D 流体流动模拟数据集，HOOI 计算 Tucker 分解的速度比 STHOSVD 快 82 倍，压缩比提高 50%。

items.title: Automatic Generation of Mappings for Distributed Fourier Operations.
items.abstract: The Fourier transform is an ubiquitous mathematical operation used in a multitude of scientific applications. Most distributed Fourier transform libraries provide rigid implementations that force developers of high performance applications to mold their code around the Fourier computation, omitting opportunities for minimizing communication across the Fourier transforms and the surrounding computation. In this work, we introduce a new automatic approach to generate distributed mappings for multi-dimensional Fourier operations, offering a solution to this problem. Our approach decides how to decompose, map, and schedule the computation as smaller and lower-dimensional parallel operations. We design and implement a novel non-linear iterative formulation that optimizes across Fourier and linear algebra operations. Our scheme leverages the Z3 SMT solver to minimize the number of communication steps across key MPI collectives, while selecting the grid shape. We evaluate the effectiveness of our new scheme and demonstrate 2 × -31 × speedups over coupled heFFTe and COSMA solutions.
items.abstract_zh: 傅里叶变换是一种在多种科学应用中普遍使用的数学运算。大多数分布式傅里叶变换库提供严格的实现，迫使高性能应用程序的开发人员围绕傅里叶计算塑造他们的代码，从而忽略了最小化傅里叶变换和周围计算之间的通信的机会。在这项工作中，我们引入了一种新的自动方法来生成多维傅立叶运算的分布式映射，为该问题提供了解决方案。我们的方法决定如何将计算分解、映射和调度为更小、更低维的并行操作。我们设计并实现了一种新颖的非线性迭代公式，可以优化傅里叶和线性代数运算。我们的方案利用 Z3 SMT 求解器来最大限度地减少关键 MPI 集合之间的通信步骤数，同时选择网格形状。我们评估了新方案的有效性，并证明了比耦合的 heFFTe 和 COSMA 解决方案提高了 2 × -31 × 的速度。

items.title: DPAR: High-Performance, Secure, and Scalable Differential Privacy-based AllReduce.
items.abstract: Secure, efficient, and scalable AllReduce-based data aggregation is essential for Artificial Intelligence (AI) and scientific applications on modern High-Performance Computing (HPC) and cloud infrastructures. As AllReduce is increasingly used across these distributed infrastructures, privacy has become a critical concern. State-of-the-art (SOTA) Homomorphic Encryption (HE)-based AllReduce solutions introduce high overhead, require secure key exchanges, and remain vulnerable to collusion. We propose DPAR, the first differentially private, collusion-resistant AllReduce framework optimized for large-scale HPC and AI workloads. DPAR introduces three key innovations: integrating Differential Privacy (DP) to eliminate collusion risks without key exchanges, scalable noise growth to preserve accuracy, and performance optimizations using a noise pooling mechanism. DPAR is a drop-in Message Passing Interface (MPI) AllReduce replacement, providing strong privacy with minimal performance cost. Evaluated on Delta and Frontier supercomputers with up to 8192 cores, DPAR outperforms the SOTA HE solution by up to 34.7% in modern AI workloads.
items.abstract_zh: 安全、高效且可扩展的基于 AllReduce 的数据聚合对于现代高性能计算 (HPC) 和云基础设施上的人工智能 (AI) 和科学应用至关重要。随着 AllReduce 越来越多地在这些分布式基础设施中使用，隐私已成为一个关键问题。基于最先进 (SOTA) 同态加密 (HE) 的 AllReduce 解决方案会带来较高的开销，需要安全的密钥交换，并且仍然容易受到串通。我们提出了 DPAR，这是第一个针对大规模 HPC 和 AI 工作负载进行优化的差分隐私、防共谋的 AllReduce 框架。 DPAR 引入了三项关键创新：集成差分隐私 (DP) 以在无需密钥交换的情况下消除共谋风险、可扩展的噪声增长以保持准确性以及使用噪声池机制进行性能优化。 DPAR 是一个嵌入式消息传递接口 (MPI) AllReduce 替代品，以最低的性能成本提供强大的隐私性。在具有多达 8192 个内核的 D​​elta 和 Frontier 超级计算机上进行评估，DPAR 在现代 AI 工作负载中的性能比 SOTA HE 解决方案高出 34.7%。

items.title: Constraint-Driven Auto-Tuning of GEMM-like Operators for MT-3000 Many-core Processor.
items.abstract: Optimizing deep learning (DL) operators, particularly GEMM-like operations, for emerging heterogeneous many-core processors like MT-3000 is challenging due to the large search space and hardware-specific constraints. Existing approaches - such as hand-crafted libraries or general-purpose auto-tuners - are either expensive to develop or deliver sub-optimal performance due to expensive search overheads. We present DynaChain, an operator-level optimization framework for MT-3000. DynaChain decouples the computation and data movement of operators, allowing each to be optimized independently and maximizing global data reuse across the operator schedule. To reduce the search space, DynaChain introduces constraint dependency chains that dynamically eliminate invalid scheduling options during exploration. It then applies an integer linear programming (ILP) based decomposition to handle irregular matrix dimensions, avoiding padding and improving hardware utilization. For low-level code generation, DynaChain offers a hardware-aware micro-kernel design optimized for the MT-3000’s VLIW+SIMD architecture, supporting irregular operations through improved register allocation and instruction pipelining. Experimental results on a range of representative DL operators demonstrate that DynaChain simplifies kernel development for heterogeneous many-core architectures while delivering performance on par with expert-optimized libraries.
items.abstract_zh: 由于巨大的搜索空间和特定于硬件的限制，针对 MT-3000 等新兴异构多核处理器优化深度学习 (DL) 算子，尤其是类似 GEMM 的算子具有挑战性。现有的方法（例如手工制作的库或通用自动调优器）要么开发成本昂贵，要么由于昂贵的搜索开销而提供次优性能。我们推出了 DynaChain，一个适用于 MT-3000 的运营商级优化框架。 DynaChain 将算子的计算和数据移动解耦，允许每个算子独立优化，并在整个算子计划中最大化全局数据重用。为了减少搜索空间，DynaChain 引入了约束依赖链，可以在探索过程中动态消除无效的调度选项。然后，它应用基于整数线性规划 (ILP) 的分解来处理不规则的矩阵维度，避免填充并提高硬件利用率。对于低级代码生成，DynaChain 提供针对 MT-3000 的 VLIW+SIMD 架构优化的硬件感知微内核设计，通过改进的寄存器分配和指令流水线支持不规则操作。一系列代表性深度学习算子的实验结果表明，DynaChain 简化了异构多核架构的内核开发，同时提供与专家优化库相当的性能。

items.title: FaSTCC: Fast Sparse Tensor Contractions on CPUs.
items.abstract: Sparse tensor contractions are a core computational primitive in scientific computing and machine learning. Effective optimization of such contractions through loop permutation/tiling remains an open challenge. Our work perform the first comprehensive comparative analysis of data access costs and memory requirements for loop permutations for sparse tensor contractions. Based on these insights, we develop FaSTCC, a novel hashing-based parallel implementation of sparse tensor contractions. FaSTCC introduces a new 2D tiled contraction-index-outer scheme and a corresponding tile-aware design. Using probabilistic modeling, our approach automatically chooses between dense and sparse output tile accumulators and selects suitable tile size. We evaluate FaSTCC across two CPU platforms and a range of real-world workloads, demonstrating significant speedups on benchmarks from FROSTT and from quantum chemistry.
items.abstract_zh: 稀疏张量收缩是科学计算和机器学习中的核心计算原语。通过循环排列/平铺有效优化此类收缩仍然是一个开放的挑战。我们的工作对稀疏张量收缩的循环排列的数据访问成本和内存需求进行了首次全面的比较分析。基于这些见解，我们开发了 FaSTCC，一种新颖的基于哈希的稀疏张量收缩并行实现。 FaSTCC 引入了一种新的 2D 平铺收缩索引外部方案和相应的平铺感知设计。使用概率建模，我们的方法自动在密集和稀疏输出瓦片累加器之间进行选择，并选择合适的瓦片大小。我们跨两个 CPU 平台和一系列实际工作负载评估了 FaSTCC，证明了 FROSTT 和量子化学基准测试的显着加速。

The work was done as part of the Reproducibility Initiative of SC25. The author is a member of the SC25 Reproducibilty Committee.
items.abstract_zh: 此再现性报告提供了有关根据 Yafan Huang 等人撰写的 SC25 论文《GPU Lossy Compression for HPC Can Be Versatile and Ultra-Fast》的工件描述和评估附录进行的工件评估的详细信息。这项工作是作为 SC25 再现性倡议的一部分完成的。作者是 SC25 重现性委员会成员。

items.title: Plexus: Taming Billion-edge Graphs with 3D Parallel Full-graph GNN Training.
items.abstract: Graph neural networks (GNNs) leverage the connectivity and structure of real-world graphs to learn intricate properties and relationships between nodes. Many real-world graphs exceed the memory capacity of a GPU due to their sheer size, and training GNNs on such graphs requires techniques such as mini-batch sampling to scale. The alternative approach of distributed full-graph training suffers from high communication overheads and load imbalance due to the irregular structure of graphs. We propose a three-dimensional (3D) parallel approach for full-graph training that tackles these issues and scales to billion-edge graphs. In addition, we introduce optimizations such as a double permutation scheme for load balancing, and a performance model to predict the optimal 3D configuration of our parallel implementation – Plexus. We evaluate Plexus on six different graph datasets and show scaling results on up to 2048 GPUs of Perlmutter, and 1024 GPUs of Frontier. Plexus achieves unprecedented speedups of 2.3 − 12.5 × over prior state of the art, and a reduction in time-to-solution by 5.2 − 8.7 × on Perlmutter and 7.0 − 54.2 × on Frontier.
items.abstract_zh: 图神经网络 (GNN) 利用现实世界图的连接性和结构来学习节点之间复杂的属性和关系。许多现实世界的图由于其庞大的尺寸而超出了 GPU 的内存容量，并且在此类图上训练 GNN 需要诸如小批量采样之类的技术来扩展。分布式全图训练的替代方法由于图的不规则结构而面临高通信开销和负载不平衡的问题。我们提出了一种用于全图训练的三维（3D）并行方法，可以解决这些问题并扩展到十亿边图。此外，我们还引入了优化，例如用于负载平衡的双排列方案，以及用于预测并行实现 Plexus 的最佳 3D 配置的性能模型。我们在六个不同的图形数据集上评估 Plexus，并在 Perlmutter 的多达 2048 个 GPU 和 Frontier 的 1024 个 GPU 上显示缩放结果。与现有技术相比，Plexus 实现了前所未有的加速 2.3 – 12.5 倍，并且在 Perlmutter 上将解决时间缩短了 5.2 – 8.7 倍，在 Frontier 上缩短了 7.0 – 54.2 倍。

The work was done as part of the Reproducibility Initiative of SC25. The author is a member of the SC25 Reproducibilty Committee.
items.abstract_zh: 该再现性报告提供了有关根据 SC25 论文 KAMI：Xiaowen Tian 等人在单个 GPU 内避免通用矩阵乘法的通信的工件描述和评估附录进行的工件评估的详细信息。这项工作是作为 SC25 再现性倡议的一部分完成的。作者是 SC25 重现性委员会成员。

items.title: Multiscale Light-Matter Dynamics in Quantum Materials: From Electrons to Topological Superlattices.
items.abstract: Light-matter dynamics in topological quantum materials enables ultralow-power, ultrafast devices. A challenge is simulating multiple field and particle equations for light, electrons, and atoms over vast spatiotemporal scales on Exaflop/s computers with increased heterogeneity and low-precision focus. We present a paradigm shift that solves the multiscale/multiphysics/heterogeneity challenge harnessing hardware heterogeneity and low-precision arithmetic. Divide-conquer-recombine algorithms divide the problem into not only spatial but also physical subproblems of small dynamic ranges and minimal mutual information, which are mapped onto best-characteristics-matching hardware units, while metamodel-space algebra minimizes communication and precision requirements. Using 60,000 GPUs of Aurora, DC-MESH (divide-and-conquer Maxwell-Ehrenfest-surface hopping) and XS-NNQMD (excited-state neural-network quantum molecular dynamics) modules of MLMD (multiscale light-matter dynamics) software were 152- and 3,780-times faster than the state-of-the-art for 15.4 million-electron and 1.23 trillion-atom PbTiO3 material, achieving 1.87 EFLOP/s for the former. This enabled the first study of light-induced switching of topological superlattices for future ferroelectric ‘topotronics’.
items.abstract_zh: 拓扑量子材料中的光物质动力学使超低功耗、超快的设备成为可能。一项挑战是在 Exaflop/s 计算机上模拟广阔时空尺度上的光、电子和原子的多个场和粒子方程，并增加异质性和低精度聚焦。我们提出了一种范式转变，利用硬件异构性和低精度算法解决多尺度/多物理/异构性挑战。分而治之的重组算法不仅将问题划分为空间子问题，还划分为小动态范围和最小互信息的物理子问题，这些子问题被映射到最佳特性匹配的硬件单元上，而元模型空间代数则最大限度地减少了通信和精度要求。使用 Aurora 的 60,000 个 GPU，MLMD（多尺度光物质动力学）软件的 DC-MESH（分治麦克斯韦-埃伦菲斯特表面跳跃）和 XS-NNQMD（激发态神经网络量子分子动力学）模块分别比最先进的 1540 万电子和 1.23 倍快 152 倍和 3,780 倍。万亿原子PbTiO3材料，前者达到1.87 EFLOP/s。这使得首次对拓扑超晶格的光诱导开关的研究成为可能，以用于未来的铁电“拓扑电子学”。

items.title: Caracal: A GPU-Resident Sparse LU Solver with Lightweight Fine-Grained Scheduling.
items.abstract: We address inefficiencies in task scheduling, memory management, and scalability in GPU-resident sparse LU factorization with a two-level approach of sequentially scheduled coarse-grained blocks containing multiple fine-grained blocks managed with a lightweight static scheduler enabling multi-stream parallelism. Additionally, we design an intelligent memory caching mechanism for the fine-grained scheduler, which retains frequently accessed data in GPU memory. To further enhance scalability, we introduce a distributed memory design that partitions the input matrix using a 1D block-cyclic distribution and optimizes inter-GPU communication via NVLink. The multi-GPU design reaches a computational throughput of 6.46 TFLOP/s on four A100 GPUs, demonstrating promising scalability. This is up to 7x speedup over the latest SuperLU_DIST with 3D communication, 94x speedup over PanguLU, 16x speedup over PasTiX, and 10x speedup over our own coarse-grained dynamic scheduling implementation while reaching up to 21% of the A100’s theoretical peak performance.
items.abstract_zh: 我们通过顺序调度粗粒度块的两级方法解决了 GPU 驻留稀疏 LU 分解中任务调度、内存管理和可扩展性方面的低效率问题，其中粗粒度块包含多个细粒度块，这些细粒度块通过轻量级静态调度程序进行管理，从而实现多流并行。此外，我们为细粒度调度器设计了智能内存缓存机制，将经常访问的数据保留在GPU内存中。为了进一步增强可扩展性，我们引入了分布式内存设计，该设计使用一维块循环分布对输入矩阵进行分区，并通过 NVLink 优化 GPU 间通信。多 GPU 设计在四个 A100 GPU 上达到了 6.46 TFLOP/s 的计算吞吐量，展现了良好的可扩展性。与具有 3D 通信功能的最新 SuperLU_DIST 相比，速度提高了 7 倍；与 PanguLU 相比，速度提高了 94 倍；与 PasTiX 相比，速度提高了 16 倍；与我们自己的粗粒度动态调度实现相比，速度提高了 10 倍，同时达到了 A100 理论峰值性能的 21%。

items.title: GreenMix: Energy-Efficient Serverless Computing via Randomized Sketching on Asymmetric Multi-Cores.
items.abstract: GreenMix is motivated by the renewed interest in asymmetric multi-core processors and the emergence of the serverless computing model. Asymmetric multi-cores offer better energy and performance trade-offs by placing different core types on the same die. However, existing serverless scheduling techniques do not leverage these benefits. GreenMix is the first serverless work to reduce energy and serverless keep-alive costs while meeting QoS targets by leveraging asymmetric multi-cores. GreenMix employs randomized sketching, tailored for serverless execution and keep-alive, to perform within 10% of the optimal solution in terms of energy efficiency and keep-alive cost reduction. GreenMix’s effectiveness is demonstrated through evaluations on clusters of ARM big.LITTLE and Intel Alder Lake asymmetric processors. It outperforms competing state-of-the-art schedulers, offering a novel approach for energy-efficient serverless computing.
items.abstract_zh: GreenMix 的动机是人们对非对称多核处理器重新产生兴趣以及无服务器计算模型的出现。非对称多核通过将不同的核心类型放置在同一芯片上，提供更好的能量和性能权衡。然而，现有的无服务器调度技术并没有利用这些优势。 GreenMix 是第一个无服务器工作，旨在通过利用非对称多核来降低能源和无服务器保持活动成本，同时满足 QoS 目标。 GreenMix 采用随机草图，专为无服务器执行和保持活动而定制，在能源效率和保持活动成本降低方面的执行效果与最佳解决方案相差 10% 以内。 GreenMix 的有效性通过对 ARM big.LITTLE 和 Intel Alder Lake 非对称处理器集群的评估得到证明。它的性能优于竞争对手最先进的调度程序，为节能无服务器计算提供了一种新颖的方法。
