items.abstract_zh: 了解科学软件的行为对于维护计算研究的完整性和透明度至关重要。跟踪不同版本的软件执行的计算参数（输入输出参数、硬件和软件的配置参数）的变化有助于通过提供上下文将结果与有意义的变化相关联并可靠地解释结果，从而有助于理解。多样化的计算环境和软件平台增加了跟踪多次运行中计算参数演变的复杂性。我们推出了 PerfAnalyzer，这是一个交互式仪表板，用于简化 Git 提交中计算参数的收集、管理和可视化分析。我们通过收集和分析 CloverLeaf 迷你应用程序的计算参数的案例研究，展示了仪表板在识别性能问题方面的有用性。案例研究的结果表明，PerfAnalyzer 能够突出显示跨版本的性能变化，并识别与使用孤立测量难以定位的变化相关的参数。

items.title: DAGonStore: Reliable Data Management for Workflows on the Computing Continuum with DynoStore and DAGonStar.
items.abstract: The computing continuum has emerged as a promising paradigm for decentralized data processing. This approach brings computation closer to data sources, reducing latency and enabling faster insights. However, managing such distributed systems introduces new challenges, particularly in ensuring the availability and reliability of data across heterogeneous and failure-prone environments. In this paper, we focus on addressing these challenges by introducing DAGonStore as a novel component of the DAGonStar workflow engine, integrating it with the DynoStore wide-area storage system to provide resilient and location-transparent data access. DAGonStore implements reliability and availability schemes based on erasure codes and utilization-aware load-balancing to guarantee that input and output data remain accessible and consistent, even in the presence of storage node failures or disconnections. We validate our approach through different tests, demonstrating that DAGonStore enables scalable and fault-tolerant workflow execution across the computing continuum with minimal user intervention.
items.abstract_zh: 计算连续体已成为去中心化数据处理的有前途的范例。这种方法使计算更接近数据源，减少延迟并实现更快的洞察。然而，管理此类分布式系统带来了新的挑战，特别是在确保异构和容易出现故障的环境中数据的可用性和可靠性方面。在本文中，我们通过引入 DAGonStore 作为 DAGonStar 工作流引擎的新颖组件，将其与 DynoStore 广域存储系统集成，以提供弹性且位置透明的数据访问，重点解决这些挑战。 DAGonStore 实施基于纠删码和利用率感知负载平衡的可靠性和可用性方案，以保证输入和输出数据保持可访问和一致，即使存在存储节点故障或断开连接。我们通过不同的测试验证了我们的方法，证明 DAGonStore 可以在整个计算连续体中以最少的用户干预实现可扩展且容错的工作流程执行。

items.title: CASSE: Targeted Threat Modeling for Data Management Libraries.
items.abstract: Data management libraries (DMLs) such as HDF5, Zarr, and NetCDF are used heavily across domains. Despite their heavy usage, security of DMLs has been sparsely explored. Threat modeling is a method for analyzing the security of complex software systems; STRIDE is the most popular model used for evaluating security of software. In this study, we evaluate the application and effectiveness of STRIDE for DMLs. We identified three key shortcomings of STRIDE when applied to DMLs: the attack categorizations are often inapplicable, the attack categories provide little context, and current approaches do not analyze file structures used by DMLs. We propose CASSE as a novel threat modeling approach targeting DMLs to focus on these problems with a new attack taxonomy and including file structure diagrams. We evaluated CASSE by using it to model threats on three popular DMLs, HDF5, NetCDF, and Zarr. The application of CASSE to other DMLs is similar.
items.abstract_zh: HDF5、Zarr 和 NetCDF 等数据管理库 (DML) 在各个领域中得到广泛使用。尽管 DML 的使用率很高，但对其安全性的探讨却很少。威胁建模是一种分析复杂软件系统安全性的方法； STRIDE 是用于评估软件安全性的最流行模型。在本研究中，我们评估了 STRIDE 在 DML 中的应用和有效性。我们发现了 STRIDE 在应用于 DML 时的三个主要缺点：攻击分类通常不适用、攻击类别提供的上下文很少、当前的方法不分析 DML 使用的文件结构。我们提出 CASSE 作为一种针对 DML 的新型威胁建模方法，通过新的攻击分类法并包括文件结构图来关注这些问题。我们通过使用 CASSE 对三种流行的 DML（HDF5、NetCDF 和 Zarr）上的威胁进行建模来对其进行评估。 CASSE 在其他 DML 中的应用是类似的。

items.title: Overcoming Dynamic I/O Boundaries: a Double-Sided Streaming Methodology with dispel4py and CAPIO.
items.abstract: This work introduces a novel double-sided streaming methodology that combines control-plane and data-plane streaming. Our goal is to implement the long-advocated separation of concerns in workflow orchestration without introducing artificial boundaries in their execution. Our approach is exemplified by the integration of control-plane streaming provided by dispel4py and the transparent data-plane streaming provided by CAPIO. Our integration eliminates file synchronization barriers without requiring modifications to existing workflow logic. To support this, we extend CAPIO with a new commit rule that allows streaming over dynamically generated file sets, enabling hybrid workflows that blend in-memory dataflows with file-based communication. We validate our approach using a real-world seismic cross-correlation workflow, achieving performance improvements between 23% and 40%. Unlike previous solutions, our method supports streaming across the entire workflow, including phase boundaries where file I/O would typically enforce strict execution ordering. Therefore, our approach can be straightforwardly extended to other multi-stage streaming applications.
items.abstract_zh: 这项工作引入了一种新颖的双面流式传输方法，该方法结合了控制平面和数据平面流式传输。我们的目标是在工作流程编排中实现长期倡导的关注点分离，而不会在执行中引入人为边界。我们的方法通过 dispel4py 提供的控制平面流和 CAPIO 提供的透明数据平面流的集成来举例说明。我们的集成消除了文件同步障碍，无需修改现有工作流程逻辑。为了支持这一点，我们使用新的提交规则扩展了 CAPIO，该规则允许流式传输动态生成的文件集，从而实现将内存中数据流与基于文件的通信混合在一起的混合工作流程。我们使用真实世界的地震互相关工作流程验证了我们的方法，实现了 23% 到 40% 的性能提升。与以前的解决方案不同，我们的方法支持跨整个工作流程的流式传输，包括文件 I/O 通常会强制执行严格执行顺序的阶段边界。因此，我们的方法可以直接扩展到其他多阶段流应用程序。

items.title: Exploring Reduced Precision for Deep Learning Activation Functions.
items.abstract: The increasing scale of deep neural networks heightens the need to optimize training and inference efficiency. Reduced-precision computation has emerged as a promising approach to improve memory usage, energy efficiency, and computational throughput. While formats such as FP16 and FP8 are increasingly supported by modern hardware for tensor operations, ultra-low-precision formats like FP4 and FP2 remain largely unexplored for non-linear activation functions, which play a critical role in model convergence and stability. This work introduces a PyTorch framework to emulate and train models where activation functions are computed using FP8, FP6, FP4, FP3, and FP2 representations throughout the training process. Through comprehensive experiments across multiple models and datasets, we demonstrate that piecewise-linear activations (ReLU variants) maintain acceptable AI accuracy even at FP2 (40-83% depending on model complexity), while smooth nonlinearities (Sigmoid, Tanh) suffer significant degradation. We identify FP4 as a practical lower bound for most activation functions, with FP6-FP8 closely matching FP32 performance across all tested configurations.
items.abstract_zh: 深度神经网络规模的不断扩大提高了优化训练和推理效率的需求。降低精度计算已成为提高内存使用、能源效率和计算吞吐量的一种有前景的方法。虽然 FP16 和 FP8 等格式越来越多地受到用于张量运算的现代硬件的支持，但 FP4 和 FP2 等超低精度格式在很大程度上尚未针对非线性激活函数进行探索，而非线性激活函数在模型收敛和稳定性方面发挥着关键作用。这项工作引入了一个 PyTorch 框架来模拟和训练模型，其中在整个训练过程中使用 FP8、FP6、FP4、FP3 和 FP2 表示来计算激活函数。通过跨多个模型和数据集的综合实验，我们证明分段线性激活（ReLU 变体）即使在 FP2 下也能保持可接受的 AI 精度（40-83%，取决于模型复杂性），而平滑非线性（Sigmoid、Tanh）则显着下降。我们将 FP4 确定为大多数激活函数的实际下限，而 FP6-FP8 在所有测试配置中与 FP32 性能非常匹配。

items.title: SmartNIC Data Exchange Framework.
items.abstract: As the field of HPC grows ever larger, now more than ever, it is important to adapt to the rapidly evolving hardware landscape, leveraging the cutting edge and advancing beyond the limits of what is considered conventional computing. Smart Network Interface Cards (SmartNICs) are one such emerging technology that have the potential to overhaul classical computing paradigms. This paper will provide an overview of a novel data exchange framework which leverages SmartNICs to gather arbitrary host data from HPC systems and exchange it via three different methods with minimal system overhead. We discuss the latency with which the framework operates, along with the ways in which its varying configurations affect performance. Finally, we provide some context as to how the field of HPC will benefit from the introduction of SmartNICs, especially as they leverage the presented framework for a variety of future applications.
items.abstract_zh: 随着 HPC 领域变得越来越大，适应快速发展的硬件环境、利用尖端技术并超越传统计算的限制变得更加重要。智能网络接口卡 (SmartNIC) 就是这样一种新兴技术，有可能彻底改变传统计算范例。本文将概述一种新颖的数据交换框架，该框架利用 SmartNIC 从 HPC 系统收集任意主机数据，并通过三种不同的方法以最小的系统开销进行交换。我们讨论框架运行的延迟，以及其不同配置影响性能的方式。最后，我们提供了一些关于 HPC 领域将如何从 SmartNIC 的引入中受益的背景信息，特别是当它们利用所提出的框架来实现各种未来应用时。

items.title: Reduction-Aware Directive-Based Programming via Multi-Dimensional Homomorphisms.
items.abstract: Directive-based programming models have established themselves as an effective and productive paradigm for exploiting parallel architectures such as GPUs and CPUs. Widely used solutions—such as OpenMP and OpenACC—are popular due to their simplicity and broad applicability to general-purpose codebases. However, these approaches often struggle to deliver consistently high and portable performance, especially for reduction-intensive computations.
items.abstract_zh: 基于指令的编程模型已成为利用 GPU 和 CPU 等并行架构的有效且高效的范例。广泛使用的解决方案（例如 OpenMP 和 OpenACC）因其简单性和对通用代码库的广泛适用性而广受欢迎。然而，这些方法通常难以提供一致的高性能和可移植性能，特别是对于缩减密集型计算。

items.title: Performance-Portable Symbolic Factorization through Common Graph Operations.
items.abstract: In lower-upper (LU) factorization of a sparse matrix in the form of A = LU, symbolic factorization is a pre-processing stage usually performed to discover the sparsity structure of the factors. A is usually not equal to L + U due to the fill-ins (the nonzeros that do not appear in A but element of L or U) introduced in the factorization. The symbolic factorization can be performed using only the pattern of A and the existing algorithms for that purpose usually utilize graphs by relying on the duality of graphs and matrices. In this work, we assess the viability of utilizing GraphBLAS for symbolic factorization. GraphBLAS defines a standard way to express operations on graphs in the language of linear algebra. We express edge-based and path-based symbolic factorization of the coefficient matrix using common graph operations and investigate utilization of masks and elimination trees for optimization. Our goal is to obtain a performant symbolic factorization, which can then be used on any hardware in a portable manner for which the GraphBLAS standard is realized. We demonstrate our approach on various non-symmetric sparse matrices by running symbolic factorization on multi-core and many-core architectures efficiently.
items.abstract_zh: 在 A = LU 形式的稀疏矩阵的下上 (LU) 分解中，符号分解是一个预处理阶段，通常用于发现因子的稀疏结构。由于分解中引入了填充（A 中未出现的非零值，而是 L 或 U 的元素），A 通常不等于 L + U。符号分解可以仅使用 A 的模式来执行，并且用于此目的的现有算法通常通过依赖图和矩阵的对偶性来利用图。在这项工作中，我们评估了利用 GraphBLAS 进行符号分解的可行性。 GraphBLAS 定义了一种用线性代数语言表达图运算的标准方法。我们使用常见的图运算来表达系数矩阵的基于边缘和基于路径的符号分解，并研究如何利用掩码和消除树进行优化。我们的目标是获得高性能的符号分解，然后可以以可移植的方式在实现 GraphBLAS 标准的任何硬件上使用。我们通过在多核和众核架构上高效运行符号分解来演示我们在各种非对称稀疏矩阵上的方法。

items.title: TEGRA - Scaling Up Graph Processing with Disaggregated Computing.
items.abstract: Graph processing workloads continue to grow in scale and complexity, demanding architectures that can adapt to diverse compute and memory requirements. Traditional scale-out accelerators couple compute and memory resources, resulting in resource underutilization when executing workloads with varying compute-to-memory intensities. In this paper, we present TEGRA, a composable, scale-up architecture for large-scale graph processing. TEGRA leverages disaggregated memory via CXL and a message-passing communication model to decouple compute and memory, enabling independent scaling of each. Through detailed evaluation using the gem5 simulator, we show that TEGRA improves memory bandwidth utilization by up to 15% over state-of-the-art accelerators by dynamically provisioning compute based on workload demands. Our results demonstrate that TEGRA provides a flexible and efficient foundation for supporting emerging graph analytics workloads across a wide range of arithmetic intensities.
items.abstract_zh: 图形处理工作负载的规模和复杂性不断增长，要求架构能够适应不同的计算和内存需求。传统的横向扩展加速器将计算和内存资源结合在一起，导致在执行具有不同计算到内存强度的工作负载时资源利用率不足。在本文中，我们提出了 TEGRA，一种用于大规模图形处理的可组合、可扩展的架构。 TEGRA 通过 CXL 和消息传递通信模型利用分解内存来解耦计算和内存，从而实现各自的独立扩展。通过使用 gem5 模拟器进行详细评估，我们发现 TEGRA 通过根据工作负载需求动态配置计算，将内存带宽利用率比最先进的加速器提高了 15%。我们的结果表明，TEGRA 为支持各种算术强度的新兴图形分析工作负载提供了灵活高效的基础。

items.title: DiOMP-Offloading: Toward Portable Distributed Heterogeneous OpenMP.
items.abstract: High-performance computing faces rising core counts, increasing heterogeneity, and growing memory bandwidth. These trends complicate programmability, portability, and scalability, while traditional MPI + OpenMP struggles with distributed GPU memory and portable performance. We present DiOMP-Offloading, a framework unifying OpenMP target offloading with a Partitioned Global Address Space (PGAS) model. Built on LLVM-OpenMP and GASNet-EX, it centrally manages global memory and supports symmetric/asymmetric GPU allocations, enabling remote put/get operations. DiOMP also integrates OMPCCL, a portable device-side collective layer that harmonizes allocation lifecycles and address translation across vendor backends. By eliminating separate MPI + X stacks and abstracting replicated device memory and communication logic, DiOMP improves scalability and programmability. Experiments on large-scale NVIDIA A100, Grace Hopper, and AMD MI250X platforms show superior micro-benchmark and application performance, demonstrating that DiOMP-Offloading offers a more portable, scalable, and efficient path for heterogeneous supercomputing.
items.abstract_zh: 高性能计算面临着核心数量不断增加、异构性不断增加以及内存带宽不断增加的问题。这些趋势使可编程性、可移植性和可扩展性变得复杂，而传统的 MPI + OpenMP 则在分布式 GPU 内存和便携性能方面遇到困难。我们提出了 DiOMP-Offloading，这是一个将 OpenMP 目标卸载与分区全局地址空间 (PGAS) 模型统一起来的框架。它基于 LLVM-OpenMP 和 GASNet-EX 构建，集中管理全局内存并支持对称/非对称 GPU 分配，从而实现远程放置/获取操作。 DiOMP 还集成了 OMPCCL，这是一个便携式设备端集体层，可协调跨供应商后端的分配生命周期和地址转换。通过消除单独的 MPI + X 堆栈并抽象复制的设备内存和通信逻辑，DiOMP 提高了可扩展性和可编程性。在大规模 NVIDIA A100、Grace Hopper 和 AMD MI250X 平台上进行的实验显示出卓越的微基准测试和应用程序性能，证明 DiOMP-Offloading 为异构超级计算提供了一条更便携、可扩展和高效的路径。

items.title: Profiling Application-Specific Properties of Irregular Graph Algorithms on GPUs.
items.abstract: With the availability of sophisticated profiling tools for GPUs such as NVIDIA’s Nsight Compute and Nsight Systems, programmers tend to overlook the level of insight that can be gained from simple profiling techniques. For instance, the basic profiling approach of manually adding counters to source code is able to expose important application-specific behavior that general-purpose profilers cannot capture. Analyzing global or thread-local counts of certain events can help developers better reason about program behaviors that are crucial for detecting performance bottlenecks, validating key assumptions, and guiding effective optimizations. In this paper, we demonstrate on the example of 5 high-performance GPU graph-analytics codes how we used this profiling approach to uncover interesting application behaviors and to develop performance optimizations based on some of them.
items.abstract_zh: 随着用于 GPU 的复杂分析工具（例如 NVIDIA 的 Nsight Compute 和 Nsight Systems）的出现，程序员往往会忽视从简单的分析技术中可以获得的洞察力。例如，手动向源代码添加计数器的基本分析方法能够公开通用分析器无法捕获的重要的特定于应用程序的行为。分析某些事件的全局或线程本地计数可以帮助开发人员更好地推理程序行为，这对于检测性能瓶颈、验证关键假设和指导有效优化至关重要。在本文中，我们以 5 个高性能 GPU 图形分析代码为例，演示了如何使用这种分析方法来发现有趣的应用程序行为，并基于其中的一些行为来开发性能优化。

items.title: Bridging the Gap: User-Centric Energy Monitoring for Policy-Driven Application Optimization in HPC Data Centers.
items.abstract: Application energy optimization in HPC data centers face two critical gaps. Systematic methodologies that connect data center policies to application decisions and accessible monitoring tools that enable data-driven optimization. We address both gaps through two complementary pillars. First, we present a methodology based on extended weighted Energy Delay Product (EDP) to translate data center operational priorities and integrate energy considerations into the energy optimization workflow which starts from continuous monitoring through targeted optimization. Second, we present a user-space monitoring tool, Omnistat, that enables this methodology by providing developers with direct access to actionable energy telemetry. Through deployment on the Frontier supercomputer and case studies exploring performance-energy trade-offs, we show how these pillars help energy as an integral optimization target for developers as active participants in data center efficiency.
items.abstract_zh: HPC 数据中心的应用能源优化面临两个关键差距。 Systematic methodologies that connect data center policies to application decisions and accessible monitoring tools that enable data-driven optimization.我们通过两个互补的支柱来解决这两个差距。首先，我们提出了一种基于扩展加权能量延迟乘积 (EDP) 的方法，用于转换数据中心运营优先级，并将能源考虑因素整合到能源优化工作流程中，该工作流程从持续监控到有针对性的优化。 Second, we present a user-space monitoring tool, Omnistat, that enables this methodology by providing developers with direct access to actionable energy telemetry.通过在 Frontier 超级计算机上的部署和探索性能与能源权衡的案例研究，我们展示了这些支柱如何帮助能源成为开发人员作为数据中心效率积极参与者的整体优化目标。

items.title: The (R)evolution of Scientific Workflows in the Agentic AI Era: Towards Autonomous Science.
items.abstract: Modern scientific discovery increasingly requires coordinating distributed facilities and heterogeneous resources, forcing researchers to act as manual workflow coordinators rather than scientists. Advances in AI leading to AI agents show exciting new opportunities that can accelerate scientific discovery by providing intelligence as a component in the ecosystem. However, it is unclear how this new capability would materialize and integrate in the real world. To address this, we propose a conceptual framework where workflows evolve along two dimensions which are intelligence (from static to intelligent) and composition (from single to swarm) to chart an evolutionary path from current workflow management systems to fully autonomous, distributed scientific laboratories. With these trajectories in mind, we present an architectural blueprint that can help the community take the next steps towards harnessing the opportunities in autonomous science with the potential for 100x discovery acceleration and transformational scientific workflows.
items.abstract_zh: Modern scientific discovery increasingly requires coordinating distributed facilities and heterogeneous resources, forcing researchers to act as manual workflow coordinators rather than scientists. Advances in AI leading to AI agents show exciting new opportunities that can accelerate scientific discovery by providing intelligence as a component in the ecosystem.然而，目前尚不清楚这种新功能将如何实现并融入现实世界。 To address this, we propose a conceptual framework where workflows evolve along two dimensions which are intelligence (from static to intelligent) and composition (from single to swarm) to chart an evolutionary path from current workflow management systems to fully autonomous, distributed scientific laboratories. With these trajectories in mind, we present an architectural blueprint that can help the community take the next steps towards harnessing the opportunities in autonomous science with the potential for 100x discovery acceleration and transformational scientific workflows.

items.title: Batch Tiling on Attention: Efficient Mixture of Experts Training on Wafer-Scale Processors.
items.abstract: Mixture of Experts (MoE) models face significant computational bottlenecks on wafer-scale processors due to conflicting batch size requirements between attention and MLP layers. While attention mechanisms demand smaller batch sizes due to memory constraints, routable MLP layers require larger batches to achieve optimal compute density on massively parallel architectures.
items.abstract_zh: 由于注意力层和 MLP 层之间的批量大小要求存在冲突，专家混合 (MoE) 模型在晶圆级处理器上面临着严重的计算瓶颈。虽然由于内存限制，注意力机制需要较小的批量大小，但可路由 MLP 层需要更大的批量才能在大规模并行架构上实现最佳计算密度。

items.title: Usability Evaluation of Cloud for HPC Applications.
items.abstract: The rise of AI and the economic dominance of cloud computing have created a new nexus of innovation for high performance computing (HPC), which has a long history of driving scientific discovery. Beyond performance needs, scientific workflows increasingly demand capabilities of cloud environments: portability, reproducibility, dynamism, and automation. As converged cloud-HPC environments emerge, there is growing need to study their suitability for HPC use cases. Here we present a cross-platform usability study that assesses 11 different HPC proxy applications and benchmarks across three clouds (Microsoft Azure, Amazon Web Services, and Google Cloud), six environments, and two compute configurations (CPU and GPU) against on-premises HPC clusters at the Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory. We perform applications scaling tests in all environments up to 28,672 CPUs and 256 GPUs. We present methodology and results to guide future study and provide a foundation to define best practices for running HPC workloads in cloud.
items.abstract_zh: 人工智能的兴起和云计算的经济主导地位为高性能计算 (HPC) 创造了新的创新纽带，长期以来，高性能计算一直在推动科学发现。除了性能需求之外，科学工作流程越来越需要云环境的功能：可移植性、可重复性、动态性和自动化。随着融合云 HPC 环境的出现，越来越需要研究它们对 HPC 用例的适用性。在这里，我们提出了一项跨平台可用性研究，针对劳伦斯利弗莫尔国家实验室的本地 HPC 集群，评估了跨三种云（Microsoft Azure、Amazon Web Services 和 Google Cloud）、六种环境和两种计算配置（CPU 和 GPU）的 11 种不同的 HPC 代理应用程序和基准。我们在最多 28,672 个 CPU 和 256 个 GPU 的所有环境中执行应用程序扩展测试。我们提出方法和结果来指导未来的研究，并为定义在云中运行 HPC 工作负载的最佳实践奠定基础。

items.title: State Machine Orchestration of an HPC Workflow in Cloud.
items.abstract: The High Performance Computing (HPC) community is facing a period of change, where access to resources is uncertain and workflows must move between available environments. The economic and innovative power of cloud presents opportunity by offering state-of-the-art orchestration frameworks like Kubernetes. However, the existence of environments does not guarantee access to them, and porting HPC applications to cloud is non-trivial. In this work, we redesign the orchestration of an ensemble-based workflow – the Multiscale Machine-Learned Modeling Infrastructure (MuMMI) for Kubernetes. We perform experiments representing a progression from a traditional MuMMI run on HPC to a fully portable variant running in cloud to assess the relative contributions of cloud-native features to workflow performance improvement. Moving from a traditional design based on service and filesystem components to an event-driven design we demonstrate 62.24% and 40.29% faster workflow completion times for CPU and GPU setups, respectively, resulting in 45.0% and 38.3% lower costs.
items.abstract_zh: 高性能计算 (HPC) 社区正面临一个变革时期，资源访问不确定，工作流程必须在可用环境之间移动。云的经济和创新力量通过提供 Kubernetes 等最先进的编排框架来提供机遇。然而，环境的存在并不能保证对它们的访问，将 HPC 应用程序移植到云中也并非易事。在这项工作中，我们重新设计了基于集成的工作流程的编排——Kubernetes 的多尺度机器学习建模基础设施 (MuMMI)。我们进行了实验，代表从 HPC 上运行的传统 MuMMI 到在云中运行的完全可移植变体的进展，以评估云原生功能对工作流程性能改进的相对贡献。从基于服务和文件系统组件的传统设计转向事件驱动设计，我们证明 CPU 和 GPU 设置的工作流程完成时间分别加快了 62.24% 和 40.29%，从而使成本降低了 45.0% 和 38.3%。

items.title: MPPI - Type safe C++ Datatypes for MPI.
items.abstract: MPI provides a flexible C-API to communicate data of various types between a set of distributed processes over high-speed interconnects in HPC systems. Data buffers are described using MPI-Datatypes, which specify the type and layout of the data to be transmitted. To construct these datatypes, users must manually describe the memory layout of buffer elements via the MPI-API. However, modern applications are typically written in object-oriented C++, which offers significant advantages over C, including type safety and metaprogramming capabilities.
items.abstract_zh: MPI 提供了灵活的 C-API，可通过 HPC 系统中的高速互连在一组分布式进程之间传送各种类型的数据。数据缓冲区使用 MPI 数据类型进行描述，它指定要传输的数据的类型和布局。要构造这些数据类型，用户必须通过 MPI-API 手动描述缓冲区元素的内存布局。然而，现代应用程序通常是用面向对象的 C++ 编写的，与 C 相比，它具有显着的优势，包括类型安全和元编程功能。

items.title: Accelerating Intra-Node GPU Communication: A Performance Model for Multi-Path Transfers.
items.abstract: Optimizing GPU-to-GPU communication is a key challenge for improving performance in MPI-based HPC applications, especially when utilizing multiple communication paths. This paper presents a novel performance model for intra-node multi-path GPU communication within the MPI+UCX framework, aimed at determining the optimal configuration for distributing a single P2P communication across multiple paths. By considering factors such as link bandwidth, pipeline overhead, and stream synchronization, the model identifies an efficient path distribution strategy, reducing communication overhead and increasing throughput. Through extensive experiments on various topologies, we demonstrate that our model accurately finds experimentally optimal configurations, achieving significant improvements in performance, with the average of less than 6% error in predicting the optimal configuration for very large messages.
items.abstract_zh: 优化 GPU 到 GPU 的通信是提高基于 MPI 的 HPC 应用程序性能的一个关键挑战，尤其是在利用多个通信路径时。本文提出了一种在 MPI+UCX 框架内节点内多路径 GPU 通信的新颖性能模型，旨在确定跨多个路径分布单个 P2P 通信的最佳配置。通过考虑链路带宽、管道开销和流同步等因素，该模型确定了有效的路径分配策略，从而减少了通信开销并提高了吞吐量。通过对各种拓扑的大量实验，我们证明我们的模型准确地找到了实验上的最佳配置，实现了性能的显着改进，在预测非常大的消息的最佳配置时平均误差低于 6%。

items.title: Architecting Tensor Core-Based Reductions for Irregular Molecular Docking Kernels.
items.abstract: Tensor Cores (TCs) are specialized hardware units designed for efficient matrix multiplication and are widely utilized in deep learning workloads. However, their adoption in more irregular high-performance computing (HPC) applications remains limited. This paper presents a methodology for effectively integrating TCs into a representative HPC application: molecular docking with AutoDock-GPU. The irregular computational patterns and strict accuracy requirements of this application pose significant challenges for TC utilization. To address these, we adopt a twofold strategy: (i) accelerating sum reduction operations using TCs, and (ii) applying state-of-the-art numerical error correction (EC) techniques to maintain accuracy. Experimental evaluations on NVIDIA A100, H100, and B200 GPUs show that our CUDA-based implementation consistently outperforms the baseline while preserving algorithmic accuracy.
items.abstract_zh: 张量核心 (TC) 是专为高效矩阵乘法而设计的专用硬件单元，广泛用于深度学习工作负载。然而，它们在更不规则的高性能计算 (HPC) 应用中的采用仍然有限。本文提出了一种将 TC 有效集成到代表性 HPC 应用中的方法：使用 AutoDock-GPU 进行分子对接。该应用的不规则计算模式和严格的精度要求给 TC 的利用带来了重大挑战。为了解决这些问题，我们采用双重策略：(i) 使用 TC 加速求和运算，(ii) 应用最先进的数值误差校正 (EC) 技术来保持准确性。对 NVIDIA A100、H100 和 B200 GPU 的实验评估表明，我们基于 CUDA 的实现始终优于基准，同时保持算法准确性。

items.title: Securing HDF5 Plugins with Digital Signatures.
items.abstract: HDF5 is a popular data management and I/O library used by numerous scientific and industry applications. HDF5 has pluggable extensions to enhance its functionality, improve performance, and utilize underlying hardware and file systems. Plugins play a crucial role in adding custom features, such as compression filters, virtual file drivers (VFDs), and virtual object layer (VOL) connectors, without requiring extensive changes to the source code or modifications to the main library. While the plugin capability enables the extension of HDF5, plugins could be misused for malicious routing of HDF5 calls. To systematically improve the security of HDF5, this study explores the option of digitally signing plugins. This would help ensure the authenticity and integrity of any plugins that users may use. We discuss a few implementation scenarios in HDF5 and assess the accuracy and overhead associated with the plugin verification process.
items.abstract_zh: HDF5 是一种流行的数据管理和 I/O 库，被众多科学和工业应用程序使用。 HDF5 具有可插拔扩展来增强其功能、提高性能并利用底层硬件和文件系统。插件在添加自定义功能（例如压缩过滤器、虚拟文件驱动程序 (VFD) 和虚拟对象层 (VOL) 连接器）方面发挥着至关重要的作用，而无需对源代码进行大量更改或对主库进行修改。虽然插件功能可以扩展 HDF5，但插件可能会被滥用来恶意路由 HDF5 呼叫。为了系统地提高 HDF5 的安全性，本研究探索了数字签名插件的选项。这将有助于确保用户可能使用的任何插件的真实性和完整性。我们讨论 HDF5 中的一些实现场景，并评估与插件验证过程相关的准确性和开销。

items.title: Connected-Component Labeling Using HLS for High-Energy Particle Physics Instruments.
items.abstract: Many instruments used in high-energy particle physics observations, e.g., gamma-ray telescopes, use FPGAs for front-end signal processing of raw sensor data. The use of high-level synthesis (HLS) to express the signal processing algorithms has the potential to significantly reduce development time for new instruments of this type. We describe our experience with one of the computational stages in the signal processing pipeline, island detection, exploring its implementation across multiple configurations: 1D versus 2D islands, and 4-way versus 8-way connected-component labeling (CCL) in the 2D configuration. We report resource usage and performance for both configurations of 2D island detection, including the required optimizations necessary for HLS to be effective. Results indicate that our implementation can perform 4-way CCL on 15k images per second even for 43 × 43 pixel arrays.
items.abstract_zh: 许多用于高能粒子物理观测的仪器（例如伽马射线望远镜）都使用 FPGA 对原始传感器数据进行前端信号处理。使用高级综合（HLS）来表达信号处理算法有可能显着缩短此类新仪器的开发时间。我们描述了我们在信号处理管道中的计算阶段之一岛检测方面的经验，探索其跨多种配置的实现：1D 与 2D 岛，以及 2D 配置中的 4 路与 8 路连通分量标记 (CCL)。我们报告两种 2D 岛检测配置的资源使用情况和性能，包括 HLS 有效所需的优化。结果表明，即使对于 43 × 43 像素阵列，我们的实现也可以每秒对 15k 图像执行 4 路 CCL。

items.title: ASaP: Automatic Software Prefetching for Sparse Tensor Computations in MLIR.
items.abstract: Sparse tensor computations suffer from irregular memory access patterns that degrade cache performance. While software prefetching can mitigate this, existing compiler approaches lack the semantic insight needed for effective optimization.
items.abstract_zh: 稀疏张量计算会受到不规则内存访问模式的影响，从而降低缓存性能。虽然软件预取可以缓解这种情况，但现有的编译器方法缺乏有效优化所需的语义洞察力。

items.title: GPU Programming for AI Workflow Development on AWS SageMaker: An Instructional Approach.
items.abstract: We present the design, implementation, and comprehensive evaluation of a specialized course on GPU architecture, GPU programming, and how these are used for developing AI agents. This course is offered to undergraduate and graduate students during Fall 2024 and Spring 2025. The course began with foundational concepts in GPU/CPU hardware and parallel computing and progressed to develop RAG and optimizing them using GPUs. Students gained experience provisioning and configuring cloud-based GPU instances, implementing parallel algorithms, and deploying scalable AI solutions. We evaluated learning outcomes through assessments, course evaluations, and anonymous surveys. The results reveal that (1) AWS served as an effective and economical platform for practical GPU programming, (2) experiential learning significantly enhanced technical proficiency and engagement, and (3) the course strengthened students’ problem-solving and critical thinking skills through tools such as TensorBoard and HPC profilers, which exposed performance bottlenecks and scaling issues. Our findings underscore the pedagogical value of integrating parallel computing into STEM education. We advocate for broader adoption of similar electives across STEM curricula to prepare students for the demands of modern, compute-intensive fields.
items.abstract_zh: 我们展示了关于 GPU 架构、GPU 编程以及如何使用它们开发 AI 代理的专业课程的设计、实现和综合评估。本课程在 2024 年秋季和 2025 年春季向本科生和研究生提供。本课程从 GPU/CPU 硬件和并行计算的基本概念开始，然后逐步开发 RAG 并使用 GPU 对其进行优化。学生获得了配置和配置基于云的 GPU 实例、实施并行算法以及部署可扩展 AI 解决方案的经验。我们通过评估、课程评估和匿名调查来评估学习成果。结果显示，(1) AWS 是实用 GPU 编程的有效且经济的平台，(2) 体验式学习显着提高了技术熟练程度和参与度，(3) 该课程通过 TensorBoard 和 HPC 分析器等工具增强了学生解决问题和批判性思维的能力，从而暴露了性能瓶颈和扩展问题。我们的研究结果强调了将并行计算融入 STEM 教育的教学价值。我们主张在 STEM 课程中更广泛地采用类似的选修课，为学生满足现代计算密集型领域的需求做好准备。

items.title: Towards Supporting QIR: Steps for Adopting the Quantum Intermediate Representation.
items.abstract: Intermediate representations (IRs) play a crucial role in the software stack of a quantum computer to facilitate efficient optimizations for executing an application on hardware. One of those IRs is the Quantum Intermediate Representation (QIR), which builds on the classical LLVM compiler infrastructure. In this article, we outline different approaches to how QIR can be adopted. This exploration culminates in a demonstration of what it takes to turn an existing quantum circuit simulator into a QIR runtime and that such a transition is less daunting than it might seem at first. We further show that switching to QIR does not entail any performance deficits compared to the original simulator. On the contrary, the presented steps effortlessly allow adding support for arbitrary classical control flow to any classical simulator. We conclude with an outlook on future directions using QIR. The implemented QIR runtime is available under https://github.com/munich-quantum-toolkit/core.
items.abstract_zh: 中间表示（IR）在量子计算机的软件堆栈中发挥着至关重要的作用，以促进在硬件上执行应用程序的有效优化。其中一种 IR 是量子中间表示 (QIR)，它构建在经典 LLVM 编译器基础架构之上。在本文中，我们概述了如何采用 QIR 的不同方法。这一探索最终展示了如何将现有的量子电路模拟器转变为 QIR 运行时，并且这种转变并不像乍看起来那样令人畏惧。我们进一步表明，与原始模拟器相比，切换到 QIR 不会带来任何性能缺陷。相反，所提出的步骤毫不费力地允许向任何经典模拟器添加对任意经典控制流的支持。最后，我们对使用 QIR 的未来方向进行了展望。已实现的 QIR 运行时可在 https://github.com/munich-quantum-toolkit/core 下找到。

items.title: Experiences of Porting Structured and Unstructured Stencil Applications to FPGA using SYCL.
items.abstract: In this paper we explore a stencil application written in SYCL on both CPU and FPGA architectures. We prepare two versions of the application, using a structured grid and an unstructured grid, and then optimise these implementations for CPU and FPGA architectures, with a focus on maintaining portability between both. We benchmark the application on an AMD CPU and an Intel Stratix 10 FPGA, seeking to answer whether we can target FPGAs productively from a single-source code base. Our findings indicate that for low arithmetic intensity kernels FPGA performance is lacking compared to CPU performance, suggesting that FPGA architectures may be unsuitable for such kernels, or that significant platform-specific optimisations may be required to reduce the performance gap, at the expense of developer productivity.
items.abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们探索了在 CPU 和 FPGA 架构上用 SYCL 编写的模板应用程序。我们使用结构化网格和非结构化网格准备了两个版本的应用程序，然后针对 CPU 和 FPGA 架构优化这些实现，重点是保持两者之间的可移植性。我们在 AMD CPU 和 Intel Stratix 10 FPGA 上对应用程序进行了基准测试，试图回答我们是否可以通过单一源代码库高效地定位 FPGA。我们的研究结果表明，对于低算术强度内核，与 CPU 性能相比，FPGA 性能较差，这表明 FPGA 架构可能不适合此类内核，或者可能需要进行重大的特定于平台的优化来缩小性能差距，但会牺牲开发人员的工作效率。

items.title: Engine-Agnostic Model Hot-Swapping for Cost-Effective LLM Inference.
items.abstract: The widespread adoption of Large Language Models (LLMs) has led to an increased demand for large-scale inference services, presenting a unique set of challenges for the HPC community. These services are characterized by moderate-scale models that require dedicating expensive GPUs to handle bursty inference requests, leading to high costs and resource underutilization. In this paper, we propose SwapServeLLM — a novel engine-agnostic hot-swapping method for cost-effective inference. This model hot-swapping approach is enabled by recent driver capabilities for transparent GPU checkpointing. SwapServeLLM optimizes resource utilization by dynamically allocating GPU resources with two key mechanisms: (1) a demand-aware preemption leveraging information about concurrent requests, and (2) efficient request routing with memory reservation minimizing inference latency. Our evaluation demonstrates that SwapServeLLM optimizes model loading for state-of-the-art inference engines by 31 × compared to vLLM and up to 29% compared to Ollama, enabling cost-effective inference.
items.abstract_zh: 大型语言模型 (LLM) 的广泛采用导致了对大规模推理服务的需求增加，为 HPC 社区带来了一系列独特的挑战。这些服务的特点是中等规模的模型，需要专用昂贵的 GPU 来处理突发的推理请求，从而导致成本高昂和资源利用不足。在本文中，我们提出了 SwapServeLLM——一种新颖的与引擎无关的热交换方法，用于经济高效的推理。这种模型热插拔方法是通过最新的透明 GPU 检查点驱动程序功能实现的。 SwapServeLLM 通过使用两种关键机制动态分配 GPU 资源来优化资源利用率：(1) 利用并发请求信息的需求感知抢占，以及 (2) 通过内存预留实现高效请求路由，最大限度地减少推理延迟。我们的评估表明，与 vLLM 相比，SwapServeLLM 将最先进推理引擎的模型加载优化了 31 倍，与 Ollama 相比高达 29%，从而实现了经济高效的推理。

items.title: A Compute Graph Simulation and Implementation Framework Targeting AMD Versal AI Engines.
items.abstract: We present a framework for developing compute graph-based applications targeting the AI Engine (AIE) array of AMD Versal SoCs. This framework enables users to embed AIE-based dataflow graph prototypes directly within existing C++ applications and automatically transform them into deployable AIE graph projects. It thereby eliminates the need to manually separate host and accelerator codebases, as required by the standard AMD Vitis workflow. The framework comprises two core components: (1) a compute graph simulation library that can be linked into existing C++ programs, and (2) a Clang-based source-to-source translator that extracts simulator-defined graphs and prepares them for compilation with AMD’s AIE toolchain. We evaluate our framework using AMD’s official example graphs and show that our generated AIE code achieves performance comparable to hand-optimized Vitis implementations. Additionally, we demonstrate how C++ compile-time code execution can be leveraged to simplify the implementation of source-to-source translation and static source analysis.
items.abstract_zh: 我们提出了一个框架，用于开发针对 AMD Versal SoC 的 AI 引擎 (AIE) 阵列的基于计算图的应用程序。该框架使用户能够将基于 AIE 的数据流图原型直接嵌入到现有的 C++ 应用程序中，并自动将其转换为可部署的 AIE 图项目。因此，无需按照标准 AMD Vitis 工作流程的要求手动分离主机和加速器代码库。该框架包含两个核心组件：(1) 一个可以链接到现有 C++ 程序的计算图模拟库，以及 (2) 一个基于 Clang 的源到源转换器，用于提取模拟器定义的图并准备好使用 AMD 的 AIE 工具链进行编译。我们使用 AMD 的官方示例图评估我们的框架，并表明我们生成的 AIE 代码实现的性能可与手动优化的 Vitis 实现相媲美。此外，我们还演示了如何利用 C++ 编译时代码执行来简化源到源转换和静态源分析的实现。

items.title: Performance portable batched linear algebra kernels for transport sweeps using Kokkos.
items.abstract: This paper describes the development of performance portable batched linear algebra kernels for SN-DG neutron transport sweeps using Kokkos. We establish a new sweep algorithm for GPUs that relies on batched linear algebra kernels. We implement an optimized batched gesv solver for small linear systems that builds upon state-of-the-art algorithms. Our implementation achieves high performance by minimizing global memory traffic and maximizing the amount of computations done at compile-time. We assess the performance of the batched gesv kernel on NVIDIA and AMD GPUs. We show that our custom implementation outperforms state-of-the-art linear algebra libraries on these architectures. The performance of the new GPU sweep implementation is assessed on the H100 and MI300A GPUs. We demonstrate that it is able to achieve high performance on both architectures, and is competitive with an optimized multithreaded CPU implementation on a 128-core AMD Genoa CPU node.
items.abstract_zh: 本文介绍了使用 Kokkos 开发用于 SN-DG 中子传输扫描的高性能便携式批量线性代数内核。我们为 GPU 建立了一种新的扫描算法，该算法依赖于批量线性代数内核。我们为基于最先进算法的小型线性系统实现了优化的批量 gesv 求解器。我们的实现通过最小化全局内存流量和最大化编译时完成的计算量来实现高性能。我们评估了批处理 gesv 内核在 NVIDIA 和 AMD GPU 上的性能。我们表明，我们的自定义实现在这些架构上优于最先进的线性代数库。新 GPU 扫描实施的性能在 H100 和 MI300A GPU 上进行评估。 We demonstrate that it is able to achieve high performance on both architectures, and is competitive with an optimized multithreaded CPU implementation on a 128-core AMD Genoa CPU node.

items.title: LLMTailor: A Layer-wise Tailoring Tool for Efficient Checkpointing of Large Language Models.
items.abstract: Checkpointing is essential for fault tolerance in training large language models (LLMs). However, existing methods, regardless of their I/O strategies, periodically store the entire model and optimizer states, incurring substantial storage overhead and resource contention. Recent studies reveal that updates across LLM layers are highly non-uniform. Across training steps, some layers may undergo more significant changes, while others remain relatively stable or even unchanged. This suggests that selectively checkpointing only layers with significant updates could reduce overhead without harming training. Implementing such selective strategies requires fine-grained control over both weights and optimizer states, which no current tool provides. To address this gap, we propose LLMTailor, a checkpoint-merging framework that filters and assembles layers from different checkpoints to form a composite checkpoint. Our evaluation indicates that LLMTailor can work with different selective checkpointing strategies and effectively reduce checkpoint size (e.g., 4.3 times smaller for Llama3.1-8B) and checkpoint time (e.g., 2.8 times faster for Qwen2.5-7B) while maintaining model quality.
items.abstract_zh: 检查点对于训练大型语言模型 (LLM) 的容错能力至关重要。然而，现有方法无论其 I/O 策略如何，都会定期存储整个模型和优化器状态，​​从而产生大量存储开销和资源争用。最近的研究表明，法学硕士各层的更新非常不均匀。在整个训练步骤中，某些层可能会发生更显着的变化，而其他层则保持相对稳定甚至不变。这表明仅对具有重大更新的层进行选择性检查可以减少开销而不损害训练。实施这种选择性策略需要对权重和优化器状态进行细粒度控制，而当前的工具还没有提供这一点。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了 LLMTailor，一个检查点合并框架，可以过滤和组装来自不同检查点的层以形成复合检查点。我们的评估表明，LLMTailor 可以使用不同的选择性检查点策略，并有效减少检查点大小（例如，Llama3.1-8B 小 4.3 倍）和检查点时间（例如，Qwen2.5-7B 快 2.8 倍），同时保持模型质量。

items.title: ModuLair: Streamlining Python Virtual Environment Management for HPC.
items.abstract: Managing Python environments on high-performance computing (HPC) systems presents unique challenges due to complex toolchains, file system constraints, and diverse user needs. We present ModuLair, a modular, metadata-driven Python virtual environment framework designed to simplify environment creation, activation, and management in HPC contexts. ModuLair supports both EasyBuild and non-EasyBuild module systems, automatically detecting to ensure reproducibility and compatibility across workflows. The framework integrates seamlessly with command-line and graphical interfaces, including the Texas A&M High Performance Research Computing (HPRC) User Support Dashboard, Drona Composer, and JupyterLab, enabling visual, intuitive environment management for both novice and experienced users.
items.abstract_zh: 由于复杂的工具链、文件系统限制和多样化的用户需求，管理高性能计算 (HPC) 系统上的 Python 环境面临着独特的挑战。我们推出 ModuLair，这是一个模块化、元数据驱动的 Python 虚拟环境框架，旨在简化 HPC 环境中的环境创建、激活和管理。 ModuLair 支持 EasyBuild 和非 EasyBuild 模块系统，自动检测以确保跨工作流程的可重复性和兼容性。该框架与命令行和图形界面无缝集成，包括 Texas A&M 高性能研究计算 (HPRC) 用户支持仪表板、Drona Composer 和 JupyterLab，为新手和经验丰富的用户提供可视化、直观的环境管理。

items.title: Modular Architecture for High-Performance and Low Overhead Data Transfers.
items.abstract: High-performance applications necessitate rapid and dependable transfer of massive datasets across geographically dispersed locations. Traditional file transfer tools often suffer from resource underutilization and instability due to fixed configurations or monolithic optimization methods. We propose AutoMDT, a novel Modular Data Transfer Architecture, to address these issues by employing a deep reinforcement learning agent to simultaneously optimize concurrency levels for read, network, and write operations. This solution incorporates a lightweight network–system simulator, enabling offline training of a Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) agent in approximately 45 minutes on average, thereby overcoming the impracticality of lengthy online training in production networks. AutoMDT’s modular design decouples I/O and network tasks, allowing the agent to capture complex buffer dynamics precisely and adapt to changing system and network conditions quickly. Evaluations on production-grade testbeds show that AutoMDT achieves up to 8 × faster convergence and a \(68\%\) reduction in transfer completion times compared to state-of-the-art solutions.
items.abstract_zh: 高性能应用程序需要在地理上分散的位置快速、可靠地传输大量数据集。传统的文件传输工具经常因固定配置或整体优化方法而遭受资源利用不足和不稳定的困扰。我们提出 AutoMDT，一种新颖的模块化数据传输架构，通过采用深度强化学习代理来同时优化读取、网络和写入操作的并发级别来解决这些问题。该解决方案采用了轻量级网络系统模拟器，平均可以在大约 45 分钟内完成近端策略优化 (PPO) 代理的离线训练，从而克服了生产网络中冗长在线训练的不切实际性。 AutoMDT 的模块化设计将 I/O 和网络任务解耦，使代理能够精确捕获复杂的缓冲区动态并快速适应不断变化的系统和网络条件。对生产级测试台的评估表明，与最先进的解决方案相比，AutoMDT 的收敛速度提高了 8 倍，传输完成时间缩短了 \(68\%\)。

items.title: IzhiRISC-V - a RISC-V-based Processor with Custom ISA Extension for Spiking Neuron Networks Processing with Izhikevich Neurons.
items.abstract: Spiking Neural Network processing promises to provide high energy efficiency due to the sparsity of the spiking events. However, when realized on general-purpose hardware – such as a RISC-V processor – this promise can be undermined and overshadowed by the inefficient code, stemming from repeated usage of basic instructions for updating all the neurons in the network. One of the possible solutions to this issue is the introduction of a custom ISA extension with neuromorphic instructions for spiking neuron updating, and realizing those instructions in bespoke hardware expansion to the existing ALU. In this paper, we present the first step towards realizing a large-scale system based on the RISC-V-compliant processor called IzhiRISC-V, supporting the custom neuromorphic ISA extension.
items.abstract_zh: 由于尖峰事件的稀疏性，尖峰神经网络处理有望提供高能效。然而，当在通用硬件（例如 RISC-V 处理器）上实现时，这一承诺可能会因重复使用基本指令来更新网络中的所有神经元而被低效代码所破坏和掩盖。此问题的可能解决方案之一是引入自定义 ISA 扩展，其中包含用于尖峰神经元更新的神经形态指令，并在现有 ALU 的定制硬件扩展中实现这些指令。在本文中，我们展示了实现基于 RISC-V 兼容处理器（称为 IzhiRISC-V）的大规模系统的第一步，该系统支持自定义神经形态 ISA 扩展。

items.title: Guiding Application Users via Estimation of Computational Resources for Massively Parallel Chemistry Computations.
items.abstract: In this work, we develop machine learning (ML) based strategies to predict resources (costs) required for massively parallel chemistry computations to guide application users before they commit to running expensive experiments on a supercomputer. By predicting application execution time, we determine the optimal runtime parameter values such as number of nodes and tile sizes. Two key questions of interest to users are addressed. The first is the shortest-time question, where the user is interested in knowing the parameter values to achieve the shortest execution time for a given problem size and a target supercomputer. The second is the cheapest-run question in which the user is interested in minimizing resource costs, e.g., finding the number of nodes and tile size that minimizes the number of node-hours for a given problem size.
items.abstract_zh: 在这项工作中，我们开发了基于机器学习 (ML) 的策略来预测大规模并行化学计算所需的资源（成本），从而在应用程序用户承诺在超级计算机上运行昂贵的实验之前为他们提供指导。通过预测应用程序执行时间，我们确定最佳运行时参数值，例如节点数量和图块大小。解决了用户感兴趣的两个关键问题。第一个是最短时间问题，用户有兴趣了解参数值，以实现给定问题大小和目标超级计算机的最短执行时间。第二个是最便宜的运行问题，其中用户对最小化资源成本感兴趣，例如，找到最小化给定问题大小的节点小时数的节点数量和图块大小。

items.title: InferA: A Smart Assistant for Cosmological Ensemble Data.
items.abstract: Analyzing large-scale scientific datasets presents substantial challenges due to their sheer volume, structural complexity, and the need for specialized domain knowledge. Automation tools, such as PandasAI, typically require full data ingestion and lack context of the full data structure, making them impractical as intelligent data analysis assistants for datasets at the terabyte scale. To overcome these limitations, we propose InferA, a multi-agent system that leverages large language models to enable scalable and efficient scientific data analysis. At the core of the architecture is a supervisor agent that orchestrates a team of specialized agents responsible for distinct phases of the data retrieval and analysis. The system engages interactively with users to elicit their analytical intent and confirm query objectives, ensuring alignment between user goals and system actions. To demonstrate the framework’s usability, we evaluate the system using ensemble runs from the HACC cosmology simulation which comprises several terabytes.
items.abstract_zh: 由于大规模科学数据集的数量庞大、结构复杂以及对专业领域知识的需求，分析大规模科学数据集提出了巨大的挑战。 PandasAI 等自动化工具通常需要完整的数据摄取，并且缺乏完整数据结构的上下文，这使得它们作为 TB 级数据集的智能数据分析助手是不切实际的。为了克服这些限制，我们提出了 InferA，这是一种多智能体系统，利用大型语言模型来实现可扩展且高效的科学数据分析。该架构的核心是一个主管代理，它协调一组专门的代理，负责数据检索和分析的不同阶段。该系统与用户进行交互，以引出他们的分析意图并确认查询目标，确保用户目标和系统操作之间的一致性。为了证明该框架的可用性，我们使用 HACC 宇宙学模拟的整体运行来评估系统，该模拟包含数 TB 数据。

items.title: A Modular, Responsive, and Accessible HPC Dashboard Built upon Open OnDemand.
items.abstract: High-performance computing (HPC) has become a critical enabler of scientific advancement across many research domains. As the HPC user community continues to expand, there is an increasing need to reduce barriers to entry and improve accessibility for researchers with varying levels of computational expertise. This paper presents a modular, responsive, and user-friendly web-based dashboard built upon the Open OnDemand framework. The system integrates several redesigned functional pages, providing streamlined access to announcements, system status, job information, and resource usage. By consolidating these capabilities into an intuitive interface with a modern design, the dashboard enhances user experience, reduces reliance on command-line tools, and facilitates more efficient interaction with HPC resources.
items.abstract_zh: 高性能计算 (HPC) 已成为许多研究领域科学进步的关键推动者。随着 HPC 用户社区的不断扩大，越来越需要减少进入壁垒并提高具有不同计算专业水平的研究人员的可访问性。本文介绍了一个基于 Open OnDemand 框架构建的模块化、响应式且用户友好的基于 Web 的仪表板。该系统集成了多个重新设计的功能页面，提供对公告、系统状态、工作信息和资源使用情况的简化访问。通过将这些功能整合到采用现代设计的直观界面中，仪表板增强了用户体验，减少了对命令行工具的依赖，并促进与 HPC 资源更有效的交互。

items.title: Bridging Performance Portability and Scalability for Plasma Simulations on Heterogeneous Systems.
items.abstract: Bridging portability and scalability is essential for HPC applications. The Vector Particle-In-Cell (VPIC) code, widely used in plasma physics simulations, historically required extensive platform-specific optimizations to achieve high performance. VPIC 2.0 addresses this challenge by adopting Kokkos for performance portability, enabling it to scale effectively across diverse architectures, including CPUs and GPUs. However, the abstractions introduced by Kokkos can obscure hardware-specific capabilities and introduce performance overhead. In this work, we mitigate these overheads by enhancing vectorization and optimizing memory access patterns through platform-targeted particle sorting in VPIC 2.0. These optimizations enable VPIC 2.0 to match the performance of the highly tuned, hardware-specific VPIC 1.2 on CPUs and to achieve superlinear scaling on GPUs.
items.abstract_zh: Bridging portability and scalability is essential for HPC applications.矢量粒子单元内 (VPIC) 代码广泛用于等离子体物理模拟，历来需要广泛的特定于平台的优化才能实现高性能。 VPIC 2.0 通过采用 Kokkos 实现性能可移植性来应对这一挑战，使其能够跨不同架构（包括 CPU 和 GPU）有效扩展。然而，Kokkos 引入的抽象可能会掩盖特定于硬件的功能并带来性能开销。在这项工作中，我们通过 VPIC 2.0 中针对平台的粒子排序来增强矢量化和优化内存访问模式，从而减轻这些开销。这些优化使 VPIC 2.0 能够与 CPU 上经过高度调优、特定于硬件的 VPIC 1.2 的性能相匹配，并在 GPU 上实现超线性扩展。

items.title: EQSIM Agent: A Conversational AI for Interactive Exploration of Large-scale Earthquake Simulation Data.
items.abstract: Large-scale earthquake simulations produce massive, high-fidelity datasets essential for seismic risk analysis, however, their volume and complexity create a barrier for researchers from various backgrounds who lack specialized knowledge and programming skills. To address this challenge, we used Large Language Models (LLMs) to develop the EQSIM Agent, a conversational AI designed for the interactive exploration of large-scale earthquake simulation data. The agent allows users to query data using natural language, receiving results such as text, images, videos, and maps. Beyond standard querying and visualization, it introduces novel features like a vision-based waveform similarity search and a Retrieval-Augmented Generation system that answers questions with facts from relevant publications. This paper details the agent’s implementation and evaluates the challenges of using LLMs in a scientific context. We also provide a practical analysis of various LLMs, evaluating their performance, tool-calling reliability, and cost, to guide the development of future scientific AI agents.
items.abstract_zh: 大规模地震模拟产生了地震风险分析所必需的大量高保真数据集，但其数量和复杂性给缺乏专业知识和编程技能的不同背景的研究人员造成了障碍。为了应对这一挑战，我们使用大型语言模型 (LLM) 开发了 EQSIM 代理，这是一种对话式 AI，专为大规模地震模拟数据的交互式探索而设计。该代理允许用户使用自然语言查询数据，接收文本、图像、视频和地图等结果。除了标准查询和可视化之外，它还引入了新颖的功能，例如基于视觉的波形相似性搜索和检索增强生成系统，该系统可以用相关出版物中的事实回答问题。本文详细介绍了该代理的实施，并评估了在科学背景下使用法学硕士的挑战。我们还提供对各种法学硕士的实用分析，评估其性能、工具调用可靠性和成本，以指导未来科学人工智能代理的开发。

items.title: Hardware-Software Co-Design of Iterative Filter-Update Numerical Methods Using Processing-In-Memory.
items.abstract: Data movement is a key bottleneck in applications such as machine learning and scientific computing. Some software techniques address this by computing on subsets of data but this still requires reading the entire dataset to determine the subset. We propose a hardware-software co-design approach for iterative methods centered around two operations–filtering and updating. We introduce a domain-specific language that supports these computational patterns to enable PIM programming. Since filter and update are simple pointwise operations, PIM hardware requires only limited compute capability.
items.abstract_zh: 数据移动是机器学习和科学计算等应用程序的关键瓶颈。一些软件技术通过计算数据子集来解决这个问题，但这仍然需要读取整个数据集来确定子集。我们提出了一种硬件-软件协同设计方法，用于围绕两个操作（过滤和更新）的迭代方法。我们引入了一种支持这些计算模式的领域特定语言来实现 PIM 编程。由于过滤和更新是简单的逐点操作，因此 PIM 硬件仅需要有限的计算能力。

items.title: Quantifying AWS S3 I/O Performance Boundaries Using the Roofline Model.
items.abstract: High-performance computing facilities increasingly form hybrid environments that integrate cloud services. To avoid cumbersome network transfers when sharing data, a new class of storage gateways map a subset of facility storage to a cloud counterpart and automatically manage data mirroring. However, the performance characteristics of accessing AWS’s S3 from HPC systems using different methods and patterns remains poorly understood. This paper presents a roofline-based analysis of three S3 integration approaches: NFS-mounted AWS Storage Gateway, data migration through Storage Gateway, and direct S3 API transfers. We extend I/O roofline modeling to characterize operational intensity and bandwidth ceilings across varying data sizes and access patterns. Our experimental evaluation demonstrates significant performance differences between access methods, with POSIX I/O on NFS Storage Gateway achieving up to 6.4× higher bandwidth than other approaches for large transfers. The roofline analysis reveals distinct characteristics for each method, enabling informed selection of S3 integration strategies.
items.abstract_zh: 高性能计算设施越来越多地形成集成云服务的混合环境。为了避免共享数据时繁琐的网络传输，一类新型存储网关将设施存储的子集映射到云对应部分并自动管理数据镜像。然而，人们对使用不同方法和模式从 HPC 系统访问 AWS S3 的性能特征仍然知之甚少。本文对三种 S3 集成方法进行了基于屋顶的分析：安装 NFS 的 AWS Storage Gateway、通过 Storage Gateway 进行数据迁移以及直接 S3 API 传输。我们扩展了 I/O 屋顶线建模，以描述不同数据大小和访问模式下的操作强度和带宽上限。我们的实验评估表明，访问方法之间存在显着的性能差异，NFS 存储网关上的 POSIX I/O 实现的带宽比其他大型传输方法高出 6.4 倍。屋顶线分析揭示了每种方法的独特特征，从而能够明智地选择 S3 集成策略。

items.title: FIRST: Federated Inference Resource Scheduling Toolkit for Scientific AI Model Access.
items.abstract: We present the Federated Inference Resource Scheduling Toolkit (FIRST), a framework enabling Inference-as-a-Service across distributed High-Performance Computing (HPC) clusters. FIRST provides cloud-like access to diverse AI models, like Large Language Models (LLMs), on existing HPC infrastructure. Leveraging Globus Auth and Globus Compute, the system allows researchers to run parallel inference workloads via an OpenAI-compliant API on private, secure environments. This cluster-agnostic API allows requests to be distributed across federated clusters, targeting numerous hosted models. FIRST supports multiple inference backends (e.g., vLLM), auto-scales resources, maintains "hot" nodes for low-latency execution, and offers both high-throughput batch and interactive modes. The framework addresses the growing demand for private, secure, and scalable AI inference in scientific workflows, allowing researchers to generate billions of tokens daily on-premises without relying on commercial cloud infrastructure.
items.abstract_zh: 我们推出了联合推理资源调度工具包 (FIRST)，这是一个跨分布式高性能计算 (HPC) 集群实现推理即服务的框架。 FIRST 在现有的 HPC 基础设施上提供对各种 AI 模型的类似云的访问，例如大型语言模型 (LLM)。该系统利用 Globus Auth 和 Globus Compute，允许研究人员通过符合 OpenAI 的 API 在私有、安全的环境中运行并行推理工作负载。这种与集群无关的 API 允许跨联合集群分发请求，针对众多托管模型。 FIRST 支持多个推理后端（例如 vLLM）、自动扩展资源、维护“热”节点以实现低延迟执行，并提供高吞吐量批处理和交互模式。该框架满足了科学工作流程中对私有、安全和可扩展的人工智能推理日益增长的需求，使研究人员能够每天在本地生成数十亿个代币，而无需依赖商业云基础设施。

items.title: Fantastic Hardware Counters and How to Find Them: Automating the Detection of Noise-Resilient Performance Counters in HPC.
items.abstract: Performance models allow examining the scaling behavior of an application and identifying performance bottlenecks at an early stage. However, application runtime measurements are often tainted by noise on HPC systems. To tackle this problem, hardware counters can be exploited. Yet, not all counters are equally suitable for performance modeling. Some are noise-sensitive, vary strongly during repeated runs, or are faulty on some systems. Thus, appropriate counters must be identified on the inspected systems, requiring extensive testing and complex setups. This paper presents an automated approach that identifies noise-resilient hardware counters on HPC systems. Our approach automatically builds the setup, runs experiments, analyzes and ranks counters based on noise resilience, and presents results via a graphical or console interface. We demonstrate our approach on two HPC clusters and show how the developed tool, alongside the proposed metrics, enables detecting noise-resilient hardware counters in HPC.
items.abstract_zh: 性能模型允许检查应用程序的扩展行为并在早期阶段识别性能瓶颈。然而，应用程序运行时测量常常受到 HPC 系统上的噪声的影响。为了解决这个问题，可以利用硬件计数器。然而，并非所有计数器都同样适合性能建模。有些对噪声敏感，在重复运行期间变化很大，或者在某些系统上出现故障。因此，必须在被检查的系统上识别适当的计数器，这需要大量的测试和复杂的设置。本文提出了一种识别 HPC 系统上抗噪声硬件计数器的自动化方法。我们的方法会自动构建设置、运行实验、分析并根据噪声恢复能力对计数器进行排名，并通过图形或控制台界面呈现结果。我们在两个 HPC 集群上演示了我们的方法，并展示了开发的工具如何与建议的指标一起检测 HPC 中的抗噪声硬件计数器。

items.title: CaRDS: Compiler-aided Remote Data Structures.
items.abstract: Far memory tiers improve memory utilization by enabling memory intensive applications to use idle memory from other machines over the network. Recently, compiler approaches to far memory have demonstrated how static analysis can be leveraged to automatically transform applications to make efficient use of remote memory tiers. However, policies in these compilers, e.g., the determination of whether objects should be remoted, prefetched, or evacuated are made conservatively at compile time or require profiling. While profiling can alleviate conservative policies, profile-guided systems can be expensive and may not work well for applications that have variation in their inputs. We propose CaRDS, system that combines both runtime and static analysis to determine far memory policies dynamically, at data structure granularity, and without profiling. CaRDS remoting policies can outperform prior automatic approaches by up to ∼ 2 × and are within 25% of profile-guided systems when the local memory is highly constrained.
items.abstract_zh: 远内存层使内存密集型应用程序能够通过网络使用其他计算机的空闲内存，从而提高内存利用率。最近，远内存的编译器方法展示了如何利用静态分析来自动转换应用程序，以有效利用远程内存层。然而，这些编译器中的策略（例如，是否应远程、预取或疏散对象的确定）是在编译时保守地制定的，或者需要进行分析。虽然分析可以缓解保守的政策，但分析引导系统可能很昂贵，并且可能不适用于输入变化的应用程序。我们提出了 CaRDS，该系统结合了运行时和静态分析，以数据结构粒度动态确定远内存策略，并且无需进行分析。当本地内存高度受限时，CaRDS 远程处理策略的性能比之前的自动方法高出 ∼ 2 倍，并且与配置文件引导系统的差距在 25% 以内。

items.title: EAS-Sim: A Framework and its Methodology for the Co-Design of Multi-Objective, Energy-Aware Schedulers for AI Clusters.
items.abstract: The explosive growth of large-scale Deep Learning (DL) models has made energy consumption a first-order operational cost and constraint in modern High-Performance Computing (HPC) datacenters. Existing DL schedulers, however, are largely single-objective and energy oblivious, struggling to balance the competing demands of performance, fairness, and Quality of Service (QoS). To address this flaw, we propose a methodology for the co-design of multi-objective and energy-aware schedulers together with the associated simulation framework, the so-called EAS-Sim. Our methodology stands as a systematic approach to enhance State-of-the-Art (SOTA) scheduling heuristics with energy-efficiency objectives.
items.abstract_zh: 大规模深度学习 (DL) 模型的爆炸性增长使能源消耗成为现代高性能计算 (HPC) 数据中心的首要运营成本和约束。然而，现有的深度学习调度程序大多是单一目标且忽视能源，难以平衡性能、公平性和服务质量 (QoS) 的竞争需求。为了解决这个缺陷，我们提出了一种多目标和能量感知调度程序与相关模拟框架（所谓的 EAS-Sim）协同设计的方法。我们的方法是一种系统方法，可增强最先进 (SOTA) 调度启发法并实现能效目标。

items.title: The MALL is Open: Exploring Shared Caches and Latency in AMD CDNA™ 3 GPUs.
items.abstract: This paper presents an analysis of memory hierarchy latency across AMD Instinct™ MI300A, MI300X, and MI250X GPUs using a fine-grained pointer-chasing microbenchmark. We characterize the scalar L1 (sL1), L2, AMD Infinity Cache™ referred to as the MALL (Memory Attached Last Level), and HBM (High Bandwidth Memory), revealing distinct latency levels and architectural trade-offs. MI300A and MI300X, based on the AMD CDNA™ 3 architecture, exhibit nearly identical latency profiles, while MI250X lacks a MALL, resulting in different performance characteristics. Memory latency remains consistent across compute partitioning modes, but NUMA Partitioning per Socket (NPS) significantly impacts performance. In NPS4 mode, partitioning improves locality, reducing latency by up to 1.42× in MALL and 1.31× in HBM. We further analyze MALL contention and Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB) behavior under varying parallelism levels, identifying conditions where MALL performance degrades. These findings provide actionable insights for optimizing memory access patterns and improving performance on AMD’s latest GPU architectures.
items.abstract_zh: 本文使用细粒度指针追踪微基准分析了 AMD Instinct™ MI300A、MI300X 和 MI250X GPU 的内存层次延迟。我们描述了标量 L1 (sL1)、L2、AMD Infinity Cache™（称为 MALL（附加最后一级内存）和 HBM（高带宽内存），揭示了不同的延迟级别和架构权衡。 MI300A 和 MI300X 基于 AMD CDNA™ 3 架构，表现出几乎相同的延迟曲线，而 MI250X 缺少 MALL，导致性能特征不同。内存延迟在不同计算分区模式下保持一致，但每插槽 NUMA 分区 (NPS) 会显着影响性能。在 NPS4 模式下，分区改进了局部性，在 MALL 中将延迟减少了 1.42 倍，在 HBM 中减少了 1.31 倍。我们进一步分析不同并行级别下的 MALL 争用和转换后备缓冲区 (TLB) 行为，识别 MALL 性能下降的条件。这些发现为优化内存访问模式和提高 AMD 最新 GPU 架构的性能提供了可行的见解。

items.title: Orchestrating Quantum-HPC Workflows with Distributed Quantum Circuit Cutting.
items.abstract: Most quantum computers today are constrained by hardware limitations, particularly the number of available qubits, causing significant challenges for executing large-scale quantum algorithms. Circuit cutting has emerged as a key technique to overcome these limitations by decomposing large quantum circuits into smaller subcircuits that can be executed independently and later reconstructed. In this work, we introduce Qdislib, a distributed and flexible library for quantum circuit cutting, designed to seamlessly integrate with hybrid quantum-classical high-performance computing (HPC) systems. Qdislib employs a graph-based representation of quantum circuits to enable efficient partitioning, manipulation and execution, supporting both wire cutting and gate cutting techniques. The library is compatible with multiple quantum computing libraries, including Qiskit and Qibo, and leverages distributed computing frameworks to execute subcircuits across CPUs, GPUs, and quantum processing units (QPUs) in a fully parallelized manner. We present a proof of concept demonstrating how Qdislib enables the distributed execution of quantum circuits across heterogeneous computing resources, showcasing its potential for scalable quantum-classical workflows.
items.abstract_zh: 当今大多数量子计算机都受到硬件限制，特别是可用量子位数量的限制，这给执行大规模量子算法带来了重大挑战。电路切割已成为克服这些限制的关键技术，它可以将大型量子电路分解为可以独立执行并随后重建的较小子电路。在这项工作中，我们介绍了 Qdislib，一个用于量子电路切割的分布式灵活库，旨在与混合量子经典高性能计算（HPC）系统无缝集成。 Qdislib 采用基于图形的量子电路表示来实现高效的分区、操作和执行，支持线切割和门切割技术。该库与多个量子计算库兼容，包括 Qiskit 和 Qibo，并利用分布式计算框架以完全并行的方式跨 CPU、GPU 和量子处理单元 (QPU) 执行子电路。我们提出了一个概念证明，展示了 Qdislib 如何实现跨异构计算资源的量子电路的分布式执行，展示了其在可扩展的量子经典工作流程方面的潜力。

items.title: Learning to Schedule: A Supervised Learning Framework for Network-Aware Scheduling of Data-Intensive Workloads.
items.abstract: Distributed cloud environments hosting data-intensive applications often experience slowdowns due to network congestion, asymmetric bandwidth, and inter-node data shuffling. These factors are typically not captured by traditional host-level metrics like CPU or memory. Scheduling without accounting for these conditions can lead to poor placement decisions, longer data transfers, and suboptimal job performance. We present a network-aware job scheduler that uses supervised learning to predict the completion time of candidate jobs. Our system introduces a prediction-and-ranking mechanism that collects real-time telemetry from all nodes, uses a trained supervised model to estimate job duration per node, and ranks them to select the best placement. We evaluate the scheduler on a geo-distributed Kubernetes cluster deployed on the FABRIC testbed by running network-intensive Spark workloads. Compared to the default Kubernetes scheduler, which makes placement decisions based on current resource availability alone, our proposed supervised scheduler achieved 34–54% higher accuracy in selecting optimal nodes for job placement. The novelty of our work lies in the demonstration of supervised learning for real-time, network-aware job scheduling on a multi-site cluster.
items.abstract_zh: 托管数据密集型应用程序的分布式云环境通常会因网络拥塞、带宽不对称和节点间数据混洗而出现速度下降的情况。这些因素通常不会被传统的主机级指标（如 CPU 或内存）捕获。在不考虑这些条件的情况下进行调度可能会导致糟糕的放置决策、更长的数据传输时间和次优的工作绩效。我们提出了一种网络感知作业调度程序，它使用监督学习来预测候选作业的完成时间。我们的系统引入了一种预测和排名机制，该机制从所有节点收集实时遥测数据，使用经过训练的监督模型来估计每个节点的作业持续时间，并对它们进行排名以选择最佳位置。我们通过运行网络密集型 Spark 工作负载，在部署在 FABRIC 测试台上的地理分布式 Kubernetes 集群上评估调度程序。与仅根据当前资源可用性做出放置决策的默认 Kubernetes 调度程序相比，我们提出的监督调度程序在选择作业放置的最佳节点方面的准确度提高了 34-54%。我们工作的新颖之处在于演示了在多站点集群上进行实时、网络感知作业调度的监督学习。

items.title: CIRE: LLVM Analysis for Floating-Point Rounding Error Affected by Precision and Optimizations.
items.abstract: We present CiRE, a tool that computes floating-point rounding error for basic blocks of LLVM codes via static analysis. Using CiRE, programmers can explore different mixed-precision settings and compiler optimizations while improving performance and guarding against excessive error. Our studies using CiRE have yielded the following insights: (1) often, performance as well as accuracy can be improved; (2) compilers for different languages produce code with widely varying error, even for the same expression; (3) the choice of subexpressions to target for low precision allocation has a huge impact on error and performance. CiRE can analyze expressions with 105 or more operators, thus making it capable of analyzing basic blocks generated by unrolling loops.
items.abstract_zh: 我们推出了 CiRE，这是一种通过静态分析计算 LLVM 代码基本块的浮点舍入误差的工具。使用 CiRE，程序员可以探索不同的混合精度设置和编译器优化，同时提高性能并防止出现过多错误。我们使用 CiRE 进行的研究得出了以下见解：(1) 通常，性能和准确性都可以得到提高； (2) 不同语言的编译器生成的代码存在很大差异的错误，即使对于相同的表达式也是如此； (3) 低精度分配目标子表达式的选择对错误和性能有巨大影响。 CiRE 可以分析具有 105 个或更多运算符的表达式，从而使其能够分析通过展开循环生成的基本块。

items.title: Seeking Cost-Optimal Infrastructure Size for Distributed Filesystems: A Ceph Case Study.
items.abstract: Distributed Filesystems (DFS) are a crucial component of modern computing environments, and their performance is critical to the success of all the facilities that rely on them. However, predicting the DFS I/O performance solely based on the storage system hardware is not trivial.
items.abstract_zh: 分布式文件系统 (DFS) 是现代计算环境的重要组成部分，其性能对于所有依赖它们的设施的成功至关重要。然而，仅根据存储系统硬件来预测 DFS I/O 性能并非易事。

items.title: In-Transit Data Transport Strategies for Coupled AI-Simulation Workflow Patterns.
items.abstract: Coupled AI-Simulation workflows are becoming the major workloads for HPC facilities, and their increasing complexity necessitates new tools for performance analysis and prototyping of new in-situ workflows. We present SimAI-Bench, a tool designed to both prototype and evaluate these coupled workflows. In this paper, we use SimAI-Bench to benchmark the data transport performance of two common patterns on the Aurora supercomputer: a one-to-one workflow with co-located simulation and AI training instances, and a many-to-one workflow where a single AI model is trained from an ensemble of simulations. For the one-to-one pattern, our analysis shows that node-local and DragonHPC data staging strategies provide excellent performance compared Redis and Lustre file system. For the many-to-one pattern, we find that data transport becomes a dominant bottleneck as the ensemble size grows. Our evaluation reveals that file system is the optimal solution among the tested strategies for the many-to-one pattern.
items.abstract_zh: 耦合的人工智能仿真工作流程正在成为 HPC 设施的主要工作负载，其日益增加的复杂性需要新的工具来进行性能分析和新的现场工作流程的原型设计。我们推出了 SimAI-Bench，这是一种旨在对这些耦合工作流程进行原型设计和评估的工具。在本文中，我们使用 SimAI-Bench 对 Aurora 超级计算机上两种常见模式的数据传输性能进行基准测试：模拟和 AI 训练实例位于同一位置的一对一工作流程，以及从一组模拟中训练单个 AI 模型的多对一工作流程。对于一对一模式，我们的分析表明，与 Redis 和 Lustre 文件系统相比，节点本地和 DragonHPC 数据暂存策略提供了出色的性能。对于多对一模式，我们发现随着整体规模的增长，数据传输成为主要瓶颈。我们的评估表明，文件系统是多对一模式的测试策略中的最佳解决方案。

items.title: OmniFed: A Modular Framework for Configurable Federated Learning from Edge to HPC.
items.abstract: Federated Learning (FL) is critical for edge and High Performance Computing (HPC) where data is not centralized and privacy is crucial. We present OmniFed, a modular framework designed around decoupling and clear separation of concerns for configuration, orchestration, communication, and training logic. Its architecture supports configuration-driven prototyping and code-level override-what-you-need customization. We also support different topologies, mixed communication protocols within a single deployment, and popular training algorithms. It also offers optional privacy mechanisms including Differential Privacy (DP), Homomorphic Encryption (HE), and Secure Aggregation (SA), as well as compression strategies. These capabilities are exposed through well-defined extension points, allowing users to customize topology and orchestration, learning logic, and privacy/compression plugins, all while preserving the integrity of the core system. We evaluate multiple models and algorithms to measure various performance metrics. By unifying topology configuration, mixed-protocol communication, and pluggable modules in one stack, OmniFed streamlines FL deployment across heterogeneous environments. Github repository is available at https://github.com/at-aaims/OmniFed.
items.abstract_zh: 联合学习 (FL) 对于边缘和高性能计算 (HPC) 至关重要，因为数据不集中且隐私至关重要。我们推出了 OmniFed，这是一个模块化框架，围绕配置、编排、通信和训练逻辑的关注点的解耦和清晰分离而设计​​。其架构支持配置驱动的原型设计和代码级覆盖按需定制。我们还支持不同的拓扑、单个部署中的混合通信协议以及流行的训练算法。它还提供可选的隐私机制，包括差分隐私 (DP)、同态加密 (HE) 和安全聚合 (SA) 以及压缩策略。这些功能通过明确定义的扩展点公开，允许用户自定义拓扑和编排、学习逻辑和隐私/压缩插件，同时保持核心系统的完整性。我们评估多种模型和算法来衡量各种性能指标。通过在一个堆栈中统一拓扑配置、混合协议通信和可插拔模块，OmniFed 简化了异构环境中的 FL 部署。 Github 存储库位于 https://github.com/at-aaims/OmniFed。

items.title: High-Performance and Power-Efficient Emulation of Matrix Multiplication using INT8 Matrix Engines.
items.abstract: Recent architectures integrate high-performance and power-efficient matrix engines. These engines demonstrate remarkable performance in low-precision matrix multiplication, which is crucial in deep learning. Several techniques have been proposed to emulate single- and double-precision general matrix-matrix multiplication (SGEMM and DGEMM, respectively) by leveraging such low-precision matrix engines. In this study, we present emulation methods that significantly outperforms conventional approaches. On a GH200 Grace Hopper Superchip, the proposed DGEMM emulation achieves a 1.4 × speedup and a 43% improvement in power efficiency compared to native DGEMM for sufficiently large problems. The proposed SGEMM emulation achieves a 3.0 × speedup and a 154% improvement in power efficiency compared to native SGEMM for sufficiently large problems. Furthermore, compared to conventional emulation methods, the proposed emulation achieves more than 2 × higher performance and superior power efficiency.
items.abstract_zh: 最近的架构集成了高性能和高能效的矩阵引擎。这些引擎在低精度矩阵乘法方面表现出卓越的性能，这对于深度学习至关重要。已经提出了几种技术来利用此类低精度矩阵引擎来模拟单精度和双精度通用矩阵乘法（分别为 SGEMM 和 DGEMM）。在这项研究中，我们提出了明显优于传统方法的仿真方法。在 GH200 Grace Hopper Superchip 上，对于足够大的问题，与原生 DGEMM 相比，所提出的 DGEMM 仿真实现了 1.4 倍的加速和 43% 的功率效率提高。对于足够大的问题，与原生 SGEMM 相比，所提出的 SGEMM 仿真实现了 3.0 倍的加速和 154% 的功率效率提高。此外，与传统的仿真方法相比，所提出的仿真实现了 2 倍以上的更高性能和卓越的功效。

items.title: A Peak Performance Model for All-to-all on Hierarchical Systems and Its Applications.
items.abstract: An accurate measure of communication performance is a key component of optimizing large scale high performance computing applications. This paper presents a model for the peak performance of all-to-all communication, in the context of systems composed of a hierarchy of interconnect bandwidths; a common trait of multi-GPU per node systems. We demonstrate an application of the model to distributed transposes, such as those encountered in distributed three-dimensional Fast Fourier Transforms. The model is validated on three different network architectures, using a variety of communication libraries, by measuring all-to-all and distributed transpose performance in a pseudo-spectral code for direct numerical simulations of 3D fluid turbulence. Both the model and the validation results provide insights into the impact of fast communication links located lower in the network hierarchy, the expected scaling for all-to-all bound problems, and performance considerations when selecting slab (1D) or pencil (2D) domain decompositions.
items.abstract_zh: 准确测量通信性能是优化大规模高性能计算应用程序的关键组成部分。本文提出了一个在由互连带宽层次结构组成的系统背景下实现全对全通信峰值性能的模型；每节点多 GPU 系统的一个共同特征。我们演示了该模型在分布式转置中的应用，例如分布式三维快速傅立叶变换中遇到的转置。该模型在三种不同的网络架构上进行了验证，使用各种通信库，通过测量伪谱代码中的全面和分布式转置性能来直接数值模拟 3D 流体湍流。模型和验证结果都提供了对位于网络层次结构中较低位置的快速通信链路的影响、所有到所有边界问题的预期扩展以及选择平板（1D）或铅笔（2D）域分解时的性能考虑因素的见解。

items.title: Distributed Sparse Tensor Computations in MLIR.
items.abstract: MLIR (Multi-Level Intermediate Representation) is a popular framework for implementing domain-specific compilers for optimizing matrix/tensor computations. However, no support currently exists in MLIR for distributed sparse tensor computations. In this paper, we describe the design and implementation of a new MLIR dialect for high-level specification of distributed sparse tensor computations. This specification is then lowered to MPI-based code for distributed execution. We illustrate the expressiveness of the new dialect using a Graph Attention Network computation with multiple sparse tensor operators.
items.abstract_zh: MLIR（多级中间表示）是一种流行的框架，用于实现特定领域编译器以优化矩阵/张量计算。然而，MLIR 目前不支持分布式稀疏张量计算。在本文中，我们描述了一种新的 MLIR 方言的设计和实现，用于分布式稀疏张量计算的高级规范。然后将该规范降低为基于 MPI 的代码以进行分布式执行。我们使用具有多个稀疏张量运算符的图注意力网络计算来说明新方言的表达能力。

items.title: Comparing Graph Algorithm Styles on NVIDIA and AMD GPUs.
items.abstract: Graph algorithms are important for many domains, and GPUs can be used to accelerate them. Unfortunately, CUDA code can only be run on NVIDIA GPUs. In this study, we port the CUDA graph codes from the Indigo3 benchmark suite to HIP. This enables them to run on both AMD and NVIDIA GPUs. In addition, it allows the study of performance differences between compiled CUDA and HIP codes that are otherwise identical. Since the Indigo3 codes are written in a variety of different implementation and parallelization styles, this also allows us to study the performance of AMD GPUs on these styles and compare the results with the NVIDIA-based style trends.
items.abstract_zh: 图算法对于许多领域都很重要，GPU 可用于加速它们。不幸的是，CUDA 代码只能在 NVIDIA GPU 上运行。在本研究中，我们将 CUDA 图形代码从 Indigo3 基准套件移植到 HIP。这使得它们能够在 AMD 和 NVIDIA GPU 上运行。此外，它还可以研究编译后的 CUDA 和 HIP 代码之间的性能差异，而这些代码在其他方面是相同的。由于 Indigo3 代码是以各种不同的实现和并行化风格编写的，这也使我们能够研究 AMD GPU 在这些风格上的性能，并将结果与​​基于 NVIDIA 的风格趋势进行比较。

items.title: Post-Variational Quantum Neural Networks on a Hybrid HPC-QC System.
items.abstract: We implement a post-variational quantum neural network on a real HPC-QC system and show the feasibility of fully training this class of algorithms on current Noisy Intermediate-Scale Quantum (NISQ) devices, which are limited by noise, low number of qubits, and scarcity. Post-variational methods are hybrid classical-quantum Machine Learning algorithms that remove the need for quantum circuits evaluations during training, thus making them more suited to the availability constraints of physical quantum devices. We investigate the scalability of the algorithm to a higher number of qubits, larger datasets, and more elaborate models, giving insight for more efficient implementation. Experiments for an image classification task on a cutting-edge HPC-QC system show that post-variational quantum neural networks are fully trainable in reasonable times on a superconducting device. The models trained also show performance at least comparable to a variational approach, with one configuration showing a significant improvement in classification accuracy.
items.abstract_zh: 我们在真实的 HPC-QC 系统上实现了后变分量子神经网络，并展示了在当前的噪声中尺度量子 (NISQ) 设备上充分训练此类算法的可行性，这些设备受到噪声、量子位数量少和稀缺性的限制。后变分方法是混合经典量子机器学习算法，无需在训练期间进行量子电路评估，从而使它们更适合物理量子设备的可用性限制。我们研究了该算法对更多数量的量子位、更大的数据集和更复杂的模型的可扩展性，为更有效的实施提供了见解。在尖端 HPC-QC 系统上进行的图像分类任务实验表明，后变分量子神经网络可以在合理的时间内在超导设备上进行完全训练。训练的模型还表现出至少与变分方法相当的性能，其中一种配置显示分类准确性显着提高。

items.title: MT4G: A Tool for Reliable Auto-Discovery of NVIDIA and AMD GPU Compute and Memory Topologies.
items.abstract: Understanding GPU topology is essential for performance-related tasks in HPC or AI. Yet, unlike for CPUs with tools like hwloc, GPU information is hard to come by, incomplete, and vendor-specific.
items.abstract_zh: 了解 GPU 拓扑对于 HPC 或 AI 中与性能相关的任务至关重要。然而，与具有 hwloc 等工具的 CPU 不同，GPU 信息很难获得、不完整且特定于供应商。

items.title: CGSim: A Simulation Framework for Large Scale Distributed Computing Environment.
items.abstract: Large-scale distributed computing infrastructures such as the Worldwide LHC Computing Grid (WLCG) require comprehensive simulation tools for evaluating performance, testing new algorithms, and optimizing resource allocation strategies. However, existing simulators suffer from limited scalability, hardwired algorithms, lack of real-time monitoring, and inability to generate datasets suitable for modern machine learning approaches. We present CGSim, a simulation framework for large-scale distributed computing environments that addresses these limitations. Built upon the validated SimGrid simulation framework, CGSim provides high-level abstractions for modeling heterogeneous grid environments while maintaining accuracy and scalability. Key features include a modular plugin mechanism for testing custom workflow scheduling and data movement policies, interactive real-time visualization dashboards, and automatic generation of event-level datasets suitable for AI-assisted performance modeling. We demonstrate CGSim's capabilities through a comprehensive evaluation using production ATLAS PanDA workloads, showing significant calibration accuracy improvements across WLCG computing sites. Scalability experiments show near-linear scaling for multi-site simulations, with distributed workloads achieving 6x better performance compared to single-site execution. The framework enables researchers to simulate WLCG-scale infrastructures with hundreds of sites and thousands of concurrent jobs within practical time budget constraints on commodity hardware.
items.abstract_zh: 全球大型强子对撞机计算网格 (WLCG) 等大规模分布式计算基础设施需要全面的仿真工具来评估性能、测试新算法和优化资源分配策略。然而，现有的模拟器面临可扩展性有限、硬连线算法、缺乏实时监控以及无法生成适合现代机器学习方法的数据集的问题。我们推出了 CGSim，这是一个用于大规模分布式计算环境的模拟框架，可以解决这些限制。 CGSim 基于经过验证的 SimGrid 仿真框架构建，为异构网格环境建模提供高级抽象，同时保持准确性和可扩展性。主要功能包括用于测试自定义工作流调度和数据移动策略的模块化插件机制、交互式实时可视化仪表板以及自动生成适合人工智能辅助性能建模的事件级数据集。我们通过使用生产 ATLAS PanDA 工作负载进行全面评估来展示 CGSim 的功能，显示整个 WLCG 计算站点的校准精度显着提高。可扩展性实验表明，多站点模拟可实现近线性扩展，与单站点执行相比，分布式工作负载的性能提高了 6 倍。该框架使研究人员能够在商用硬件的实际时间预算限制内模拟具有数百个站点和数千个并发作业的 WLCG 规模的基础设施。

items.title: StreamHub: High-performance Managed SciStream as a Service.
items.abstract: Scientific data streaming enables the real-time transfer, processing, and analysis of high-throughput experimental data, providing low-latency insights that are essential for adaptive experiment control. We present SciStream-as-a-Service (StreamHub), a secure, high-performance, and scalable framework that integrates Globus Compute with SciStream to deliver continuous memory-to-memory data transfer, in-transit processing, and robust zero-trust security while orchestrating the entire streaming setup across distributed facilities with only approximately 4.3 s overhead. We describe StreamHub’s design, core components, and deployment model, and evaluate its performance under diverse configurations. Our results demonstrate that StreamHub can be deployed without privileged access, ensures end-to-end encryption and authentication across institutions, and achieves near line-rate throughput with under 2% overhead compared to unencrypted transfers, making it a practical solution for real-time scientific discovery and experiment steering.
items.abstract_zh: 科学数据流可以实时传输、处理和分析高通量实验数据，提供对于自适应实验控制至关重要的低延迟见解。我们推出了 SciStream-as-a-Service (StreamHub)，这是一个安全、高性能且可扩展的框架，它将 Globus Compute 与 SciStream 集成在一起，以提供连续的内存到内存数据传输、传输中处理和强大的零信任安全性，同时在分布式设施中编排整个流设置，开销仅为约 4.3 秒。我们描述了 StreamHub 的设计、核心组件和部署模型，并评估了其在不同配置下的性能。我们的结果表明，StreamHub 可以在没有特权访问的情况下进行部署，确保跨机构的端到端加密和身份验证，并以与未加密传输相比不到 2% 的开销实现接近线速的吞吐量，使其成为实时科学发现和实验指导的实用解决方案。

items.title: Scalable Neural Network Training: Distributed Data-Parallel Approaches.
items.abstract: Training large neural networks is computationally demanding and often limited by synchronization overhead in distributed environments.
